title: Profile and reduce repeated Reflaxe.C whole-program build cost
description: The exact pinned Haxe 5 compilation-server experiment produced byte-identical Caxecraft artifacts but no meaningful end-to-end speedup, even for an unchanged split build to the same output directory. Keeping a process alive can reuse some Haxe frontend work, but every request still reaches Reflaxe.C whole-program capture, HxcIR lowering/validation, declaration/project planning, maintainability-facing emission, and the output transaction. We need phase attribution before choosing incremental plans or caches.
design: Instrument first and keep timings opt-in or test-owned so normal compiler output remains deterministic. Do not cache raw mutable Haxe compiler objects across requests. Prefer immutable source facts or validated plans keyed by complete semantic inputs only after proving their construction dominates. If target lowering rather than Haxe typing dominates, optimize that pipeline instead of adding more server plumbing. Treat split/package/unity as separate define contexts unless one-typing/multi-layout emission is deliberately designed and parity-gated.
acceptance_criteria: Add deterministic low-overhead phase timing that separates Haxe parse/type/cache work from typed-program capture, HxcIR construction/validation, analyses, CAST/project planning, printing, and output ownership; compare at least three cold and same-context warm Caxecraft builds on controlled/recorded host conditions; identify the dominant repeated phase with executable evidence; implement the smallest safe optimization only if it measurably improves repeat wall time; preserve byte-identical cold/warm artifacts, complete source/file invalidation, stale-file deletion, diagnostics, and fresh mutable CompilationContext state; retain cold recovery and do not enable unsafe Haxe server no-check policies.
notes:
Implemented opt-in request-local compiler phase timing and a 3-cold/3-post-prime-warm Caxecraft profiler with exact-pin, artifact-parity, host-load, and macOS Haxe timer-clock evidence. Source audit found three HxcIR-to-CAST body traversals on the ordinary acyclic path. Production now builds one normal body; the body fixture explicitly opts into the #line copy, and project planning re-emits only when non-returning call-cycle semantics require it. Post-change contended sample: cold median 18.408s, warm median 16.486s, 30 normal artifacts byte-identical; remaining dominant CAST body construction was 11.441s cold and 11.652s warm. Haxe server reuse is therefore retained for future dev/watch work as a roughly 10 percent edit-loop win, while direct cold builds remain the recovery/CI baseline. Created haxe_c-xge.30 for the remaining emitter bottleneck so it does not delay the next playable Caxecraft increment. Passing so far: profiler 3+3 parity, formatter, typed boundaries, all-sources, body-lowering, function-lowering recursive/layout/native lanes, project-layout, and Caxecraft Eval/split/package/unity/native O2 differential.
Final local verification also passed the playable Caxecraft snapshot/direct-C lane and full Governance (114 tests). Profiler documentation and report now explicitly state that its 18.4s/16.5s boundary ends at generated C and excludes Clang/GCC, native linking, Raylib, and game execution.
close_reason: Added opt-in exact target-phase profiling, proved byte-identical 3-cold/3-warm Caxecraft output, and removed two redundant HxcIR-to-CAST body traversals from the ordinary path while preserving explicit line-mapped fixtures and non-returning-cycle re-emission. Post-change medians are 18.408s cold and 16.486s warm under recorded contention; the server provides a measured roughly 10 percent edit-loop benefit. All focused compiler/layout/native/Caxecraft/playable/Governance gates pass. The remaining 11.4-11.7s CBodyEmitter cost is bounded by haxe_c-xge.30 and will not block Caxecraft feature progress.

title: Reuse one Haxe compilation server in the Caxecraft edit lane
description: The default Caxecraft domain gate launches a fresh Haxe process for the Eval oracle and each split/package/unity C render. Under shared-host load, an otherwise healthy package render exceeded its 90-second request timeout during pre-commit. The compiler already proves request-local warm-server behavior, but the normal edit loop uses HAXE_NO_SERVER=1 and does not benefit from it.
design: Keep Eval isolated because its target facts differ from the C target. Start one loopback-only ephemeral Haxe --wait process for the three normal C rendering requests, pass --connect explicitly, and terminate/reap it on every success or failure path. Confirm against the pinned Haxe source that cache contexts are define-signature-aware and files are invalidated by modification checks; do not enable unsafe no-check policies. Retain cold builds in the exhaustive determinism lane and compare cold/server artifacts byte-for-byte. Measure cold and server-backed runs before selecting the default. If different layout defines prevent a meaningful improvement, retain the server for same-layout dev/watch rebuilds and pursue one-typing/multi-layout compiler emission separately rather than claiming a speedup.
acceptance_criteria: The default edit lane and future watch/build path use one owned ephemeral server where measurement proves it faster; no orphan process or fixed-port collision survives success, timeout, SIGINT, or validation failure; generated files, HxcIR, manifests, snapshots, and native traces match cold output exactly; rejected requests do not leak compiler state; an explicit cold authority remains in the full lane; focused governance tests assert process/request ownership and cleanup; timing docs state what the server can and cannot cache; before/after timings and host load are recorded without claiming an unsupported p95.
notes: Implemented and real-probed an exact-pin native Haxe 5 server/client path: HaxeShim resolve-args retains scoped dependency resolution, while the native server binds only 127.0.0.1 on an ephemeral port and is reaped on success, failure, or interrupt. Byte-for-byte artifacts and HxcIR matched cold builds. Measurement rejected using it in the default edit gate: at starting load 9.66, cold split/package/unity totaled 147.026s while one server totaled 186.413s (-26.8% in this contended diagnostic sample); same-output split repeats at starting load 18.32 were flat at 52.759s, 52.933s, and 56.853s. Hosted evidence is also nearly flat (four cold 269.783s vs four warm 266.312s). Retain warm requests as determinism/state-isolation evidence, keep the normal lane cold, and move optimization to phase-attributed repeated target work rather than claiming process reuse is faster.
close_reason: Completed the conditional server evaluation and safe transport. The runner can now own an exact-pin native Haxe 5 server bound to ephemeral loopback, resolves scoped HXML through locked HaxeShim, reaps on success/failure/interrupt, and preserves cold artifacts/HxcIR byte-for-byte. Measurement rejected promotion into the default lane: cold three-layout 147.026s versus server 186.413s in one contended sample; same-output repeats stayed flat; hosted cold/warm evidence is also nearly equal. Warm requests remain exhaustive determinism/state-isolation evidence, default stays cold, and haxe_c-fbq owns phase-attributed target-pipeline optimization. Focused lifecycle tests, real native handshake, three-layout parity benchmark, CI/toolchain policy, Governance 111 tests, and docs pass.

title: Restore pinned-toolchain CI headroom after exhaustive Caxecraft QA
description: Governance run 29696206056 proved every focused/platform job green, but the serial pinned-toolchain umbrella reached its 30-minute cap during the final centrally owned snapshot regeneration. The log shows all suites through full Caxecraft passed; snapshots:check was still making progress when GitHub cancelled the job. Preserve the complete gate and set a measured timeout with reasonable headroom.
design: The original containment-only option was to raise the serial timeout. After the user explicitly required fast tooling as a directive and asked for GitHub CI setup, replace the serial mega-job with five isolated, exact-coverage toolchain shards and a stable fail-closed pinned-toolchain aggregate. Keep every canonical command and the independent central snapshot proof; use 30-minute per-shard containment and a 5-minute aggregate timeout. Verify the replacement Governance run completes every shard and platform job.
acceptance_criteria: The CI policy requires five exact toolchain shards, bounded per-shard and aggregate timeouts, and the stable fail-closed pinned-toolchain status; an executable partition guard proves every test:toolchain command appears exactly once and the canonical serial command remains intact; policy/governance tests pass locally; a replacement Governance run completes every shard plus native GCC/Clang, Raylib, formatting, provenance, and secret scanning.
close_reason: Replaced the serial 30-minute pinned-toolchain mega-job with five exact-coverage isolated GitHub shards plus the stable fail-closed aggregate. The canonical 38-command local lane and all snapshot/native/platform gates remain intact. Local evidence: npm run deps:verify, npm run test:governance (63 governance tests), exact shard coverage, CI/fixture policy, YAML validation, staged path/Gitleaks guards, and the complete pre-commit compiler/snapshot/native chain all passed. Hosted evidence: Governance run https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29698408759 passed all 17 jobs in 8m07s; shard durations were objects 4m48s, Caxecraft 6m14s, contracts 6m35s, snapshots 7m17s, semantics 7m59s, and aggregate 3s. Snapshot compute de-duplication remains explicitly owned by haxe_c-xge.25.

title: Normalize the raylib core selection hash across CRLF checkouts
description: The required Windows clang-cl raylib desktop job checks out docs/specs/raylib-core-selection.json with CRLF bytes, while scripts/raylib/core_binding.py compares a raw-byte SHA-256 recorded from the LF checkout. The parsed closed JSON is identical, but validation fails with raylib core selection hash is stale before compilation. Preserve exact raw-byte hashes for upstream C headers; make only the repository-owned textual selection digest line-ending-stable, with fail-closed UTF-8/JSON validation and no weakening of semantic drift detection.
acceptance_criteria: LF and CRLF encodings of the same valid selection produce the existing locked digest; any semantic byte change still fails the lock; malformed UTF-8/JSON still fails closed; focused raw-core/provisioning tests pass; required Windows clang-cl, Linux, and macOS raylib jobs pass without changing the reviewed selection or upstream header identity.
notes:
Discovered by Governance run 29685203819: Linux GCC and macOS Apple Clang raylib desktop lanes passed, but Windows clang-cl failed before compilation because the repository selection JSON raw-byte digest changed under CRLF checkout conversion.
Implemented a dedicated selection_sha256 text boundary that normalizes LF/CRLF/legacy-CR before hashing while leaving upstream header/archive/license hashes exact. validate_lock now accepts an explicit selection path for adversarial tests. Focused 12-test core binding suite, full 27-test raylib provisioning lane, governance 63-test lane, and git diff --check pass. The reviewed selection and lock digest did not change.
Remote acceptance is complete: Governance run 29685749885 passed all 12 jobs at commit 1c7d00d, including the previously failing Raylib 6.0 desktop windows-clang-cl job, Linux GCC, macOS Apple Clang, headless raylib, pinned toolchain, both native smoke and build-adapter lanes, provenance, formatting, and full-history secret scanning.
close_reason: Normalized only repository-owned raylib selection JSON line endings before hashing while preserving exact upstream byte identities. Added CRLF, semantic-drift, and malformed-UTF-8 regression tests. Local focused/full raylib, governance, and all 30 snapshots passed; Governance run 29685749885 passed every job including Windows clang-cl.

title: Enforce a generated-C maintainability rubric on showcase programs
description: Current snapshot and native gates prove bytes, compilation, execution, ABI, and determinism but do not fail when correct output degenerates into an unreadable CFG dump. Define executable, reviewable output-quality evidence and apply it to Caxecraft plus smaller canonical fixtures.
design: Use a small typed/reporting rubric rather than subjective prose alone. Measure source-to-output ownership, ordinary-name readability, digest/encoding incidence, temporary density, label/goto density by justified category, function/file size, and source mapping. Pair metrics with representative hand-written C baselines and structural assertions; do not reward semantic unsafety or game-specific formatting. Store reviewed thresholds and first offending coordinates in a deterministic report.
acceptance_criteria: A documented schema-validated report is emitted or generated by QA for Caxecraft and representative canonical fixtures; CI fails on unexplained regressions in name, temporary, goto, file, or function complexity; every allowed goto is categorized; snapshots cover split and unity shapes without deriving expected bytes from the printer; strict native/Eval behavior remains the overriding gate; the README/capability inventory does not call output showcase-quality until the rubric passes.
notes: Implemented the bounded generated-C maintainability contract. Replaced the old project-wide unique-name regex with a comment/string-safe lexer, closed report and policy schemas, exact ownership/function/source coordinates, independent declaration/reference budgets, name classifications aligned with the compiler's 12-64 hex collision suffix contract, typed goto authorities, and first-violation coordinates. QA now covers body lowering, evaluation-order production plus the complete 6 irreducible + 1 loop-switch goto taxonomy, Caxecraft split/unity exact snapshots and replay/parity, and two durable handwritten calibration kernels. Caxecraft records 53 functions, 514 temporary declarations, 757 references, 262 declarations per 1000 function code lines, 50-byte maximum identifier, and zero gotos. Validation passed the complete 38-command two-worker toolchain matrix; full Caxecraft O0/O2/ASan+UBSan; final focused body, evaluation, default Caxecraft, exact native-only replay/parity, 88 governance tests, snapshot catalog, Haxe formatter, schema/JSON/Python checks, and staged secret/whitespace scans. Fresh final review approved after fixing expanded collision suffix recognition and native-only split/unity parity. The reviewer was GPT-5 Codex family, so strict different-family review remains unavailable and is not claimed.
close_reason: Added and validated the closed generated-C maintainability rubric across body lowering, evaluation order, and Caxecraft split/unity output. Exact snapshots/replay, typed goto provenance, reviewed budgets, handwritten calibration, native/Eval correctness, full toolchain, governance, formatting, and fresh review all pass; claims remain explicitly bounded to the fixed corpus.

title: Preserve readable source identities and safely coalesce generated values
description: Caxecraft output exposes semantic hashes, encoded internal role strings, anonymous structural aggregate identities, and a stable C temporary for every load. The naming registry is collision-safe, but the chosen semantic bases and unconditional stabilization obscure source concepts. Preserve source-owned naming facts through lowering and introduce narrowly proven CAST-level value coalescing.
design: Capture module/type/typedef/field/function/local display identities before type expansion loses them. Allocate readable deterministic C names first and add compact suffixes only for real collisions, specializations, anonymous values, or length limits. Keep full semantic keys in reports, not ordinary spellings. Analyze validated HxcIR uses and mutation/effect barriers; inline or directly initialize only pure, total, nonvolatile, single-use values whose sequencing, aliasing, lifetime, and failure behavior is proven. Preserve stabilization around C operand/argument-order gaps and observable loads.
acceptance_criteria: Named Haxe records/classes/enums/functions/fields/locals produce recognizable C names; collision-free Caxecraft output has no long digest or zx-encoded internal-role spelling; genuine collisions remain deterministic and are explained in hxc.symbols.json; focused adversarial tests prove no delayed loads, reordered calls, volatile/foreign alias errors, lifetime changes, or lost failures; output-complexity metrics bound temporary/name noise; GCC/Clang O0/O2, Eval parity, warm/reordered determinism, and existing symbol/evaluation-order gates pass.
notes: Use the checked-in Caxecraft BlockCoord header as the concrete readability fixture. A collision-free named record emits a short path-derived guard, the recognizable hxc_caxecraft_domain_BlockCoord tag, and short hxc_x/hxc_y/hxc_z members. The hxc_ prefix is intentional compiler-owned namespace protection: the C preprocessor expands macros before C scope lookup, so bare source names such as bool, NULL, or NAN are unsafe in generated declarations. Full semantic keys remain in hxc.symbols.json; ordinary C spellings receive a compact hash suffix only for a real collision, anonymous identity, specialization, or length limit. Generated-file provenance may remain a short comment.
close_reason: Hosted run 29712727888 exposed a native-only fixture regression after readable symbols replaced the old hardcoded spelling. Added a registered, schema-validated native-symbols.json projection generated from hxc.symbols.json, and made both full and checked-in native replay consume that stable source-to-C mapping. Local native-only Clang O0/O2, full static initialization, exact snapshot, fixture/CI policy, formatter, and all 70 governance tests pass. Implementation is complete; final true GCC/Clang hosted validation will be observed before handoff.

title: Structuralize reducible HxcIR control flow before CAST emission
description: CBodyEmitter currently maps every HxcIR jump, branch, and switch edge directly to labels and gotos. That preserves semantics but makes ordinary Haxe if/while/switch code look like a compiler CFG dump. Add a validated structuralization boundary so common reducible regions become idiomatic C without collapsing or weakening HxcIR.
design: Analyze the validated function CFG and build a closed typed region plan for sequences, if/else, loops, switches, returns, and explicit escape edges. Emit structured CAST for proven regions. Permit goto only for shared cleanup/error exits, irreducible graphs, or a documented bounded case; never infer semantics from generated label spelling. Verify region coverage, dominance/post-dominance assumptions, block-parameter/value availability, completion, and cleanup ownership before emission. Fail closed or retain the validated fallback only where policy explicitly permits it.
acceptance_criteria: Focused fixtures cover nested if/else, while, do/while-equivalent shapes, break/continue, switches without fallthrough, early returns, short-circuit expressions, failure/cleanup exits, and an irreducible synthetic CFG; reducible functions contain no blanket block labels/gotos and the irreducible/cleanup cases use only documented edges; HxcIR dumps remain semantically unchanged; strict GCC/Clang O0/O2, Eval differential, sanitizer/UB, determinism, and negative malformed-region tests pass; the printer receives structural legal input and performs no semantic repair.
close_reason: Implemented and verified typed reducible-CFG structuralization before CAST. Ordinary generated control flow is now source-shaped and Caxecraft is enforced zero-goto; bounded switch-loop escapes and proven irreducible CFGs retain explicit authority. Full npm test, all 30 snapshots, Clang O0/O2/sanitizers, Caxecraft differential/determinism, formatting, and governance passed. GCC identity remains covered by CI because local gcc resolves to Clang.

title: Implement deterministic split and unity generated-C layouts
description: The production emitter currently funnels almost every reachable definition into include/hxc/program.h and src/program.c even though lowered functions retain module ownership and the typed project boundary supports multiple GeneratedFile values. Add explicit project layout selection and make source-shaped split output the human-facing default while preserving an amalgamated unity option.
design: Use one closed typed layout setting with at least split and unity variants. Split groups definitions by normalized Haxe module/package ownership, emits deterministic private headers/prototypes from complete-type/linkage dependencies, and keeps a small entry unit. Unity renders the same finalized declaration/function plan into one implementation unit. File assignment is a pure plan before printing; build manifests and output ownership consume the resulting artifact set. Recursive-cycle partitioning must be reconciled with both layouts rather than bypassed.
acceptance_criteria: A representative multi-package Haxe fixture and Caxecraft emit predictable source-shaped paths in split mode and a single implementation unit in unity mode; both consume the same validated HxcIR and symbol/representation plans; byte output is stable across root, locale, discovery order, repeated and warm-server builds; all headers compile independently where declared; GCC/Clang O0/O2 executables and Eval traces match in both modes; build manifests enumerate the exact selected files; switching layouts safely removes stale owned artifacts and preserves user files; documentation explains tradeoffs and defaults.
notes:
Implemented one typed semantic/declaration plan with split-default and unity file assignment. Split emits deterministic package/module headers and sources, a common representation header, small entry/support units, exact build manifests, portable path encoding, and safe stale ownership. Added multi-package split/unity QA and moved Caxecraft snapshots to the split tree. Caxecraft now has a fast local O2 lane and an exhaustive determinism/O0/O2/ASan+UBSan lane retained by test:toolchain and native-only CI. Verification: npm run test:toolchain passed all gates and 30 snapshot suites; npm run test:governance passed 63 tests; formatter and focused quick/full Caxecraft gates passed.
Independent review found and this change now fixes one false-cycle risk: complete type definitions retain hard owner-header edges, but aggregate/class/tagged-enum references in prototypes and extern declarations use common forwards; native enums remain hard because strict C11 cannot forward-declare them. HardRecord/SoftRecord proves the C-valid mixed hard/soft cycle under standalone GCC/Clang headers, split/unity, determinism, stale cleanup, and Eval parity. Package-coalesced HXC-COMP-011 scope is now explicitly owned by haxe_c-xge.18.5. The older full-toolchain note is superseded; closure still waits for the final stable-state full gate and refreshed review.
Final stable-state evidence now replaces the superseded pre-review run: npm run test:toolchain passed every compiler/runtime/native/differential gate, project-layout, full Caxecraft split+unity O0/O2/ASan+UBSan, and all 30 snapshot suites. Post-run npm run test:governance, npm run format:haxe:check, git diff --check, capability regeneration/check, and exact Caxecraft asset validation all passed. Fresh-context GPT-5 Codex same-family review reports no blocking flags. Structured control flow, readable names, rubric enforcement, and package-coalesced layout remain separately owned by haxe_c-xge.18.2, .18.3, .18.4, and .18.5.
close_reason: Implemented and verified deterministic split-default and unity generated-C layouts over the same validated semantic/declaration plans. Source-shaped module ownership, common forwards, hard/soft dependency legality, standalone headers, exact manifests, stale cleanup, cold/reverse/locale/warm determinism, GCC/Clang O0/O2 behavior, Eval parity, and Caxecraft full sanitizer parity all pass. Deferred readability and package-coalescing remain explicitly owned by haxe_c-xge.18.2-.18.5.

title: Deliver human-maintainable, source-shaped generated C
description: Caxecraft exposed that the validated production path emits one IR-shaped program.c with long internal names, a temporary for every load, and basic-block labels/gotos for ordinary structured Haxe. This is semantically honest but does not satisfy the product contract that generated C be readable, idiomatic, and useful to a C programmer who wants to inspect or continue the project. Close the gap in the general compiler; do not prettify checked-in snapshots or add Caxecraft-specific emitter branches.
design: Keep TypedExpr -> validated HxcIR as the semantic correctness boundary. Add a separately owned HxcIR-to-CAST presentation/legalization layer that preserves semantics while selecting readable names, structured reducible C control flow, proven-safe value coalescing, and deterministic project layout. Support split and unity layouts from the same finalized program. Retain labels/gotos only for documented cleanup/error exits, irreducible graphs, or another mechanically justified C pattern.
acceptance_criteria: The Caxecraft generated project is readable enough to map modules, declarations, functions, fields, and control flow back to Haxe without consulting HxcIR IDs; split and unity outputs compile and behave identically; ordinary reducible if/loop/switch source does not become blanket label/goto code; collision-free source names do not carry hashes or encoded internal roles; every eliminated temporary has an executable sequencing proof; generated-output rubric, deterministic snapshots, strict GCC/Clang O0/O2, Eval parity, runtime/ABI reports, and stale-file ownership gates pass; no raw C or example-specific compiler branch is introduced.
notes: All five children are complete. The production HxcIR-to-CAST path now emits readable source identities, structurally reconstructs ordinary reducible control flow instead of blanket gotos, safely coalesces proven values, enforces a generated-C maintainability rubric, and supports deterministic split, package, and unity layouts from one semantic plan. Unity remains the single src/program.c option. Caxecraft is the flagship product-level E2E/QA workload and retains exact snapshots, Eval/native parity, strict O0/O2 and sanitizer coverage, runtime-plan evidence, deterministic output, exact ownership/stale cleanup, and remote cold-snapshot verification. Package completion shipped in dcbb2e30227a201ae9ed851a76582f288e5a5fa0; Governance 29729525446 and Cold snapshot audit 29729525464 passed. Future readability or performance findings belong to their focused owners rather than keeping HXC-COMP-011 open.
close_reason: Completed the human-maintainable generated-C milestone across all five children. Generated C now preserves readable source identities, structurally emits ordinary reducible control flow, coalesces values only with sequencing proof, enforces the showcase maintainability rubric, and offers split, package, or one-file unity output from the same validated semantic pipeline. Caxecraft and focused fixtures prove deterministic snapshots, strict native compilation, runtime parity, sanitizer behavior, manifests, stale cleanup, and zero example-specific/raw-C shortcuts. Package completion is dcbb2e30227a201ae9ed851a76582f288e5a5fa0; remote Governance 29729525446 and Cold snapshot audit 29729525464 passed.

title: Decide whether reflaxe.c can safely pin Haxe 4.3.7
description: The user prefers stable Haxe 4.3.7 over Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1. Audit the actual 4.3.7 compiler source and executable behavior before changing the reviewed dependency contract. Determine whether 4.3.7 can activate reflaxe.c without inheriting Cross target.utf16/utf16 facts or losing the scalar UTF-8/custom-target platform contract; assess Reflaxe and macro API compatibility, stdlib surface drift, lifecycle/server behavior, provenance, and any explicit fork or carrier decision that would be required.
design: Do not treat this as a version-string edit. Preserve fail-closed target identity and ADR 0004 string semantics. If upstream 4.3.7 cannot satisfy the contract, present the smallest explicit alternatives and their costs instead of silently patching Haxe or relabeling another target.
acceptance_criteria: Record source-anchored and executable evidence for 4.3.7 activation/platform behavior; compare the current Haxe 5 custom-target path; decide upstream pin, explicit maintained fork/backport, alternate supported carrier, or retention of the preview pin; update/author the governing ADR; if migration is admitted, update every pin/provenance artifact and regenerate platform/stdlib snapshots; run package, cold/server lifecycle, typed stdlib, all-source, target, and full relevant gates.
close_reason: Re-audited official Haxe 4.3.7 source and executable behavior. It has no custom-target/setPlatformConfiguration path, falls through to Cross with unremovable UTF-16 facts, and current sources also use Haxe 5-only AccPrivateCall. Recorded alternatives and retained the exact Haxe 5 preview carrier; any Haxe 4 fork requires a new owner-approved ADR. Verified: direct 4.3.7 help/Cross/all-source probes; npm run deps:verify; npm run test:bootstrap; npm run test:hxc-ir; npm run test:governance-policy.

title: Make tracked Git hook wrappers propagate repository-hook failures
description:
Context: While committing E1.T08, scripts/hooks/pre-commit failed its all-source gate, but the tracked .beads/hooks/pre-commit wrapper continued into Beads integration and returned success. The same unchecked delegation exists in pre-push. This can allow a commit or push after a repository-owned policy gate fails.

Scope: Make both tracked Beads hook wrappers immediately return the exact nonzero status from their delegated repository hook, and add executable governance evidence that later Beads behavior cannot mask that result.

Non-scope: Changing which repository checks run, weakening Beads timeout behavior, or redesigning hook installation.

Discovered while completing haxe_c-jdo.
acceptance_criteria:
Both .beads/hooks/pre-commit and .beads/hooks/pre-push stop immediately and return the delegated scripts/hooks status when it is nonzero.
Executable tests run each wrapper in an isolated fake checkout, force the delegated hook to fail, and prove the Beads phase is not invoked.
Security/governance guards fail if failure propagation is removed.
The successful hook chain and public preflight remain green.
close_reason: Fixed the fail-open wrapper defect. Both tracked .beads hook wrappers now capture the exact nonzero status returned by scripts/hooks/pre-commit or scripts/hooks/pre-push and exit before Beads integration can mask it. scripts/ci/check_security_tooling.py now executes each wrapper in an isolated fake checkout, forces delegated statuses 73 and 74, verifies argument forwarding, and proves the fake bd command is never invoked. Evidence passed: sh -n .beads/hooks/pre-commit .beads/hooks/pre-push; python3 -m py_compile scripts/ci/check_security_tooling.py; python3 scripts/ci/check_security_tooling.py; npm run test:governance (57 tests); npm run public:preflight (formatter, 92-commit Git scan, decoded history for 148 Beads issues, governance); the real pre-commit and corrected pre-push chains; GitHub Governance run 29541071365. The repository-hook selection and Beads timeout/database behavior are intentionally unchanged.

title: [SECURITY] Harden repository for public visibility
description: Audit every reachable Git ref and GitHub repository surface before publication. Match or exceed the leak prevention used by the sibling public Haxe targets, pin CI actions and security tooling, enforce Haxe formatting and staged/full-history secret scans, verify private reporting, then change visibility only after all local gates pass.
acceptance_criteria: Tracked content and all reachable Git history pass Gitleaks and local-path review; active pre-commit formats Haxe and scans staged content; pre-push and CI scan full history with checksum-verified tooling; every external GitHub Action is commit-pinned; common credential files are ignored; GitHub secrets, variables, environments, artifacts, branches, issues, PRs, and releases are audited; repository becomes public only after the audit; private vulnerability reporting is enabled and SECURITY.md is updated after verification.
close_reason: Completed the public-repository security preflight and transition. Commits df1b6c3, 0668ad3, and 7112cba add exact Haxe formatter enforcement; staged, complete Git-ref, and decoded Beads-history Gitleaks gates; checksum-verified Gitleaks 8.30.0; full-commit GitHub Action pins; credential ignores; guarded Dolt publication; governance tests; and verified public disclosure documentation. Audits found no leaks across 9 MB of Git history including refs/dolt/data, 80.17 MB of decoded history for 147 Beads issues, and 7.84 MB of downloadable Actions logs; the two unavailable logs were zero-step billing failures. GitHub reports PUBLIC, zero secret alerts, empty Actions/Dependabot/Codespaces secret and variable stores, and no environments, deploy keys, webhooks, artifacts, releases, issues, or PRs. Secret scanning, push protection, Dependabot alerts/security updates, private vulnerability reporting, and read-only Actions defaults are enabled and verified. Optional validity and non-provider pattern scanning remain unavailable and are not claimed. npm audit found zero vulnerabilities; public Governance run 29528732210 passed all six jobs. No compiler, runtime, generated-C, or public-ABI behavior changed.

title: [HXC-DISC] Resolve the production Haxe carrier for C semantics
description:
Context: Pinned Haxe 4.3.7 uses its Cross platform when Reflaxe runs without a native Haxe output target. Cross irreversibly initializes target.name=cross, target.utf16/utf16, target.sys, static, and related platform facts before initialization macros. The public macro API can add or overwrite values but cannot remove a define, so relabeling Cross as C would type UTF-16 stdlib and application branches contrary to ADR 0004/0007.

Scope: Choose and implement a production Haxe carrier whose compiler configuration matches reflaxe.c semantics, or explicitly supersede the affected owner decisions with measured compatibility consequences. Evaluate at least an exact Haxe 4 compiler patch/fork, a Haxe version with supported custom targets, and a documented Cross-plus-overrides strategy.

Non-scope: Implement the C String runtime, broad stdlib parity, or relax the UTF-8 scalar contract silently.

Validation: Run an actual non-probe reflaxe.c typing invocation and structural stdlib report. Prove static C-appropriate typing, target.name=c, c, target.unicode, absence of target.utf16/utf16, no unrelated carrier conditional, and adapter-derived sys/thread/atomic capabilities under cold and compiler-server builds.

Compatibility: Preserve ordinary Haxe conditionals and fail closed until the selected carrier is reproducible on every supported development host. Any compiler fork or version move must update the immutable toolchain/provenance contract.

Documentation: Record the accepted alternative in ADR 0007 or a superseding ADR and reconcile toolchain, configuration, architecture, PRD, AGENTS, and downstream Beads evidence.

## Rationale

Compiler platform configuration controls nullability, scoping, capture, exceptions, standard-library precedence, Unicode units, and capability conditionals before Reflaxe lowering. A target-name-only rewrite would make reports look correct while preserving contradictory typed branches, so production bootstrap cannot proceed without an explicit carrier decision and executable evidence.

## Alternatives Considered

- Build and provenance-pin an exact Haxe 4.3.7 compiler patch that adds a C platform configuration. This could preserve the language pin but creates cross-platform compiler distribution and maintenance obligations.
- Move to a Haxe version with supported custom-target configuration. This could provide the cleanest upstream API but requires compatibility review of Haxe/Reflaxe/compiler behavior and a new immutable pin.
- Keep Haxe 4 Cross and override affected standard-library modules/conditionals. This minimizes compiler distribution work but cannot hide global cross/UTF-16 facts from arbitrary portable application source and is therefore not acceptable without a superseding compatibility decision.
- Use Eval or another built-in target as the production carrier. Current evidence rejects this as a silent import of unrelated type-system, stdlib, and capability semantics; Eval remains a test oracle only.
acceptance_criteria: An accepted decision names the production carrier and rejected alternatives; the exact pinned toolchain supplies a static C-appropriate platform configuration; actual target typing exposes target.name=c, c, and target.unicode without target.utf16/utf16 or an unrelated carrier conditional; sys/thread/atomic facts come only from the resolved adapter; upstream String/StringTools/UnicodeString branch snapshots and cold/server probes pass; E0.T04 remains blocked until this evidence is executable.
close_reason: Accepted exact official Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1 at revision 2c1e544e0a2c7524ef4c8e103f1b0580362ea538 as the production typing carrier. Direct builds use --custom-target c=<output>; automatic c.Init.init()/TargetPlatform configure a static, block-scoped, scalar-Unicode PlatformConfig before typing, derive sys only for hosted, and advertise no threads/atomics without adapter evidence. CompilerInit validates that snapshot and derives Reflaxe c_output from Compiler.getOutput(), rejecting missing or mismatched output. Actual non-probe registration reaches source-root HXC1000 and emits no artifact in cold and compiler-server builds; lifecycle, staged-package, non-C isolation, recovery, hosted/freestanding, and upstream String/StringTools/UnicodeString snapshots pass. ADR 0007, ADR 0004, PRD, README, AGENTS, architecture, configuration, toolchain, provenance, research, and checked plan now agree. Rejected Haxe 4 Cross because unremovable UTF-16 facts leak to application conditionals; rejected a Haxe 4 fork because of cross-host distribution/maintenance burden; retained Eval only for future hxc bootstrap, differential oracle, and isolation. Exact official platform artifact hashes are lock-checked. Evidence: npm test; npm run deps:verify; jq empty docs/specs/*.json; python3 -m py_compile scripts/ci/check_toolchain.py test/bootstrap/run.py test/typed_c/run.py; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh; bd lint --json (0); bd dep cycles --json ([]); git diff --check.

title: [HXC-DISC] Validate C target identity and Unicode platform defines
description:
Context: ADR 0007 chooses c_output as the Haxe 4/Reflaxe activation signal, c/target.name=c as public identity, target.unicode without target.utf16, and capability-derived platform defines. The partial checkout cannot yet prove that these signals activate exactly once or select the intended Haxe 4.3.7 stdlib branches.

Scope: Under the pinned Haxe/Reflaxe toolchain, add focused macro/type-check probes for c_output and target.name activation, non-C no-op behavior, public/internal define visibility, Unicode/UTF-16 selection, and conflicting platform definitions. Record the resolved compiler configuration and typed upstream String/StringTools branches.

Non-scope: Implement the C string runtime, Unicode algorithms, or broad stdlib parity; E4.T03/E5 own those.

Validation: Positive and negative HXML/macro fixtures show exactly-once activation, c public identity, reflaxe_c implementation-only guidance, target.unicode=true, target.utf16=false, and source-positioned rejection of conflicting identities/capabilities. The pinned target-owned Haxe bootstrap type-check gate runs the probes reproducibly.

Compatibility: Ordinary Haxe conditionals use #if c; c_output stays a path-bearing build transport. Capability flags are never asserted merely because the target is C.

Documentation: Reconcile ADR 0007, configuration/agent guidance, and E0.T03/E0.T04 evidence if implementation constraints require a superseding decision.
acceptance_criteria: c_output and target.name=c activation probes initialize the compiler exactly once; non-C builds remain untouched; c and Unicode platform conditions select the documented Haxe 4.3.7 branches; conflicting identity/UTF-16/capability inputs fail at a source position; the result is integrated into the reproducible bootstrap type-check gate.
notes:
Created from E0.T01 as the deferred experiment required before bootstrap hardens ADR 0007. It blocks E0.T04 and is blocked by E0.T03; it does not own String runtime semantics or broad stdlib work.
E0.T03 established the lifecycle subset under the exact pin: cold and compiler-server probes cover c_output activation, non-C isolation, exactly-once registration guards, public c, implementation-only reflaxe_c, target.unicode, and absence/rejection of target.utf16. This discovery remains open for target.name/custom-target activation, conflicting identity and capability inputs, source-positioned diagnostics, and proof of the selected upstream Haxe stdlib branches.
Resolved evidence: CompilerInit now normalizes target.name=c only after carrier validation, records exactly-once bootstrap/init counts and the raw carrier, adds root-anchored HXC0003 conflicts, and never treats caller -D c as activation. test/bootstrap/expected/target-contract.json proves c_output and compiler-owned target.name activation, public c versus internal reflaxe_c, target.unicode without target.utf16/utf16, and the scalar typed fields from pinned upstream String/StringTools/UnicodeString under the explicit Eval lifecycle carrier. Negative probes cover reserved target.* CLI input, unrelated identity/carrier, UTF-16, atomics, freestanding capability leakage, and the actual Haxe 4 Cross carrier. Cross predefines unremovable UTF-16 facts, so production bootstrap remains fail-closed and decision haxe_c-od2.6 now blocks E0.T04 and E4.T03.
close_reason: Acceptance complete for HXC-SCAF-003, HXC-SCAF-007, and HXC-SEM-006 target-identity validation. CompilerBootstrap/CompilerInit now expose deterministic count=1 markers, validate the raw Haxe carrier before normalizing target.name=c, preserve c_output as transport and c as the public conditional, retain reflaxe_c as implementation-only, add target.unicode without adding capability flags, and issue HXC0003 at the compilation root for conflicting identity, UTF-16, unproven atomics, or freestanding carrier facts. test/bootstrap/TargetContractProbe.hx plus expected/target-contract.json structurally prove the pinned upstream String.hx, StringTools.hx, and UnicodeString.hx scalar branches, target.utf16=false/utf16=false, target.name=c, public/internal visibility, c_output and compiler-owned target.name activation, caller -D c non-activation, non-C isolation, duplicate idempotence, package/CWD behavior, and valid/invalid/valid compiler-server isolation. The experiment also proved a material pin constraint: real Haxe 4.3.7 Cross predefines target.name=cross and unremovable target.utf16/utf16 before macros. Production Cross now fails closed instead of typing the wrong program; new P0 decision haxe_c-od2.6 evaluates a pinned Haxe 4 patch, a supported custom-target Haxe version, or a superseding compatibility choice and blocks E0.T04/E4.T03. ADR 0004/0007, PRD, architecture, configuration, toolchain, research, README, AGENTS, diagnostic catalog, CI, pre-commit, and live downstream notes agree that Eval is a test carrier only and its sys/threaded facts are not C capability claims. Evidence: npm test; npm run test:bootstrap after final formatting; jq empty docs/specs/*.json test/bootstrap/expected/target-contract.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json; python3 -m py_compile test/bootstrap/run.py test/typed_c/run.py; bd dep cycles --json => []; bd lint --json => zero; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh; git diff --check.

title: [HXC-DISC] Restore typed C authoring surface and macro contract
description:
Context: The partial checkout lacks the target-facing C abstractions named by the PRD, while the product contract requires C developers to author headers, declarations, layout, linkage, ownership, and other C-native facts through typed Haxe surfaces.

Scope: Ratify the Haxe-first C authoring and DSL-admission contract; restore the minimal compilable c.* types, metadata, and macro seeds needed by E0.T04; and map deeper semantics to E3, E6, E7, E8, and E9 owners.

Non-scope: Complete bindgen, full ABI export, every pointer operation, or application DSL implementations. Those remain with their existing epics.

Validation: The pinned Haxe toolchain type-checks macro and runtime branches; positive and negative fixtures prove source-positioned validation; generated declarations remain structural and deterministic; runtime reports prove no hidden hxrt use.

Compatibility: Ordinary Haxe remains portable. c.* imports and C metadata are explicit target boundaries. Public header and ABI spellings remain provisional until the owning ADR and fixtures accept them.

Documentation: Update ADR 0002, PRD Section 17, architecture/configuration guidance, diagnostics, and the dependent Beads acceptance criteria.
acceptance_criteria: Document justified Haxe equivalents for C declarations, header grouping, includes, qualifiers, layout, linkage, calling conventions, compile-time constants/assertions, and build facts; define an admission gate that requires typed inputs and outputs, source-positioned diagnostics, deterministic expansion evidence, explicit unsafe/runtime effects, and a reason each macro or DSL exists; restore a minimal c.* scaffold that E0.T04 can type-check; add or update downstream issue ownership without normalizing raw C injection.
notes: Ratified M0 surface: method-free compiler contracts under std/c; canonical namespaced @:c.* metadata; per-compilation TypedCContractSnapshot with forward/complete dependencies, deduplicated build facts, and explicit empty runtime effects; c.StaticAssert.require admitted; c.Syntax/c.Unsafe remain non-operational. Downstream ownership notes updated on E0.T04, E0.T06, E1.T01/T03/T04, E3.T01, E6.T03/T04/T05/T09, E7.T01/T02/T03, E8.T04/T07, E9.T06/T08, and E10.T09.
close_reason: Acceptance complete for the M0 contract slice of HXC-MAC-001, HXC-MAC-002, HXC-MAC-003, HXC-MAC-007, HXC-MAC-010, HXC-MAC-011, and HXC-MAC-012. Added 42 method-free std/c compiler contracts; canonical namespaced @:c.* metadata; per-compilation TypedCContractSnapshot/TypedCContractMacro with structural type refs, forward/complete declaration edges, normalized/deduplicated build facts, explicit effects, and HXC5002 validation; admitted compile-time-only c.StaticAssert.require while c.Syntax/c.Unsafe remain non-operational. docs/typed-c-authoring.md, ADR 0002, PRD §17/§32, architecture, configuration, research, README, AGENTS, durable Beads vocabulary, and downstream live issue notes now agree. Positive snapshot plus 11 negative fixtures prove typed enum/literal admission, paths/build names, reserved/duplicate symbols, pack shape, opaque by-value rejection, cycle rejection, missing args, static assertions, root-order determinism, valid/invalid/valid compiler-server isolation, and empty allocation/unsafe/runtime effects. Evidence: npm test; jq empty docs/specs/*.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json; python3 -m py_compile test/typed_c/run.py test/bootstrap/run.py; bd dep cycles --json => []; bd lint --json => zero; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh and --staged; git diff --check; targeted trailing-whitespace scan. Actual c.* lowering, generated headers/native assertions, Clang ABI authority, public ABI stability, hxc inspection, and whole-program runtime plans remain with the documented E0/E1/E3/E4/E6/E7/E8/E9/E10 owners.

title: [HXC-DISC] Create private GitHub repository and truthful README
description:
Requirement IDs: HXC-SCAF-001, HXC-SCAF-006
PRD sections: §22, §29, §32, §36

Context: The owner requested an initial private GitHub home for the reflaxe.c scaffold and a basic compelling README.

Scope: Create fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c as a private repository with gh; set the local origin; revise README.md to communicate the vision, current M0 status, core contracts, and honest next steps; commit and push the existing in-scope scaffold.

Non-scope: Ratifying the final public license, claiming unsupported compiler capabilities, implementing compiler/runtime features, or opening a pull request.

Validation: Verify repository visibility and default branch through gh; verify README links and claims against the checkout; run available scaffold gates; push Git and Beads state when the configured remotes support them.

Compatibility: Documentation and repository hosting only; no generated-C, runtime, source-language, or public-ABI behavior changes.

Documentation: README.md and remote repository metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- GitHub repository fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c exists and remains private until the owner explicitly authorizes public visibility.
- origin points to the repository and the local default branch is pushed.
- README.md is concise, compelling, explicit that this is an M0 scaffold, and accurately explains optional hxc ergonomics plus Eval/native bootstrapping.
- Canonical GPL-3.0-only text is checked in and README/PRD license language agrees.
- A tracked, installable pre-commit chain exports Beads state, rejects staged secrets/local paths/whitespace, formats staged Haxe, and runs relevant scaffold checks without disabling other Beads hooks.
- Existing scaffold and Beads state are committed without fabricating absent capabilities.
- Validation and publication evidence are recorded in the close reason.
notes:
Owner decision: retain hxc as a later optional ergonomics/orchestration layer for C-specific native toolchain, bindgen, export, doctor, and ABI inspection workflows. Direct Haxe/HXML compilation remains canonical; hxc is not a Reflaxe requirement and must not become an opaque custom build system. Document this distinction in README.md and PRD Section 18.
Publication-readiness scope added by owner: keep GitHub visibility private for now; add GPL-3.0-only; port the proven sibling pre-commit security/path/formatting/Beads pattern with checks grounded in this scaffold.
close_reason: Requirements HXC-SCAF-001 and HXC-SCAF-006 satisfied. Artifacts: private https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c on main; README.md; LICENSE (GPL-3.0-only); .gitleaks.toml; scripts/hooks/install.sh; scripts/hooks/pre-commit; scripts/lint/local_path_guard_staged.sh; scripts/security/run-gitleaks.sh; chained .beads/hooks/pre-commit; docs/PRD.md Section 18 and architecture CLI bootstrap contract; deferred dogfood issue haxe_c-lxi.1. Publication commits: f476a72, eb5d85f, ac646eb. Validation: gh repo view confirmed PRIVATE visibility, main default branch, and GNU GPLv3 license detection; scripts/hooks/install.sh succeeded with core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks; .beads/hooks/pre-commit passed end to end; staged and history scripts/security/run-gitleaks.sh scans reported no leaks; bash -n and sh -n passed; git diff --cached --check passed; jq empty passed for all current JSON specs; strict C11 and C++17 public-header checks passed; bd dep cycles --json returned []; bd lint --json returned zero findings; bd orphans --json returned null; Git main and Beads refs/dolt/data were both published. Repository remains private pending explicit owner authorization to make it public.

Default and explicit --dry-run previews require no bd executable and write neither .beads nor .hxc. Apply uses non-interactive argument arrays, creates epics before parented tasks and bulk blocking edges, preserves open/claimed/closed execution status, recovers through the atomic ignored state map, stable metadata/external references, or exact titles, and verifies the resulting bd ready task set before replacing state. Evidence: npm run test:beads-plan passed 6 focused tests; npm run test:governance passed 26 governance tests; npm test passed the full compiler, 14-snapshot, native, and governance matrix; scripts/hooks/pre-commit passed on the exact staged diff; python3 -m py_compile scripts/beads/*.py test/governance/test_beads_bootstrap.py; jq empty docs/specs/*.json package.json; and git diff --check. Live reconciliation with python3 scripts/beads/bootstrap.py --apply --json returned createdIssues=0, reusedIssues=133, createdBlockingEdges=0, reusedBlockingEdges=274, and a ready set consistent with current closed/claimed state. A disposable real Beads repository recovered an interrupted partial import to the complete 133-issue/274-edge graph with initial ready E0.T01. After closing E0.T01 and deleting local recovery authority, repeat apply returned createdIssues=0, reusedIssues=133, createdBlockingEdges=0, reusedBlockingEdges=274, with ready E0.T02, E0.T03, E0.T06, and E0.T08 and no duplicates. bd dep cycles --json returned []; bd lint --json returned zero findings; bd orphans --json returned null. title: [HXC-E2] Primitive semantics, control flow, and UB discipline
description:

Milestone: M2

Execution: Children may proceed in parallel unless an explicit blocking edge says otherwise.

## Success Criteria

- core fixtures compile and execute;
- differential semantic suite passes;
- sanitizer suite passes;
- `hello` contains no unused broad runtime feature.
close_reason: Completed the E2 primitive-semantics, control-flow, and UB-discipline milestone after all 12 child issues closed with executable evidence. The bounded vertical slice now covers typed primitive representation and conversions, constants/locals/blocks/returns, static functions and calls, explicit Haxe evaluation order, structured control flow, UB-safe arithmetic, string literals and minimal printing, fixed arrays and spans, deterministic static initialization, and the ordinary-Haxe hello program. Core fixtures compile and execute at O0/O2; the reproducible pinned-Eval differential corpus passes; supported-host GCC/Clang ASan/UBSan evidence is clean; and hello retains only four narrow source-reasoned runtime features with no broad object, GC, reflection, Dynamic, or exception slice. Governance https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29567247435 passed the pinned toolchain, formatter, provenance, full-history scan, both Linux native compiler families, and both build-adapter families. This closes only the M2 primitive/control-flow vertical slice: general Haxe and standard-library support, broad runtime/platform claims, the hxc CLI, exceptions/Dynamic/objects, and public ABI stability remain with their owning beads.

title: [HXC-E2-T07] Lower string literals and minimal trace/Sys.println
description:

PRD sections: §12.3, §14.4

Context: Support immutable literals and the minimal hosted output path needed by hello without pulling the full object runtime.

Scope: Support immutable literals and the minimal hosted output path needed by hello without pulling the full object runtime.

close_reason: Completed E2.T07 in commit d930e9a. Compiler-known immutable String literals now lower through schema-3 HxcIR with validated Unicode-scalar UTF-8 bytes and an independently checked byte length; embedded NUL remains explicit logical content in both HxcIR and structural strict C11. Literal-only hosted Sys.println and default trace emit hxc_io_println calls with native-status abort edges. The helper writes the exact logical byte range, appends a newline, flushes stdout, returns HXC_STATUS_IO_ERROR on write or flush failure, and generated callers abort on every non-OK status. Runtime planning records one source-root reason per call and packages exactly runtime-base, status, string-literal, and io: base.h, status.h, string_literal.h, io.h, and io.c. Allocator, full string operations, object, GC, dynamic, reflection, exception, ABI-query, and status-name slices stay absent. Primary evidence: test/string_output and its positive, negative, snapshot, runtime, and differential case manifests; test/runtime/runtime-feature-graph; runtime/hxrt/include/hxrt/{string_literal,io,status}.h; runtime/hxrt/src/io.c; test/string_output/expected/{output.hxcir,program.c,runtime-plan.json,stdlib-report.json}; capability and stdlib ledgers; and the C++17 public-header consumer. Validation passed: npm test; npm run public:preflight; bash scripts/hooks/pre-commit; npm run snapshots:check; npm run test:string-output; npm run test:runtime-features; npm run test:native; and git diff --check. Compatibility: portable auto and metal minimal use identical literal semantics with their documented diagnostics; runtime none fails closed with HXC2000; non-hosted Sys.println remains rejected; primitive-only builds remain runtime-free; generated output advances only the bounded private runtime closure; the provisional internal marker is 0.4.0 and no stable public C ABI is claimed. General String values, Sys.print, custom trace position data, and broader I/O remain exact HXC1001 non-scope.

close_reason: Completed E1.T07 and HXC-COMP-009/011/012 with a typed filesystem-independent CProjectEmissionPlan, closed artifact/configuration variants, validated SHA-256 GeneratedFile values, deterministic UTF-8 ordering, merged build-fact provenance, finalized symbol/runtime/ABI/stdlib/manifest sidecars, and a fail-closed ReflaxeOutputWriter. The writer prevalidates old ownership and the complete new set, rejects traversal, existing and dangling output-root/descendant symlinks, duplicate or malformed ownership entries, and unowned collisions before delegating writes and stale removal to OutputManager. Executable evidence in test/project_emitter proves byte identity across absolute roots and discovery order, verified content hashes, unchanged-file mtime preservation, safe stale cleanup, user-file survival, no partial output on failures, and the unchanged production HXC1000 boundary; strict native lanes independently compile every emitted header and link/run the structural C11 project. Root AGENTS.md now requires explicit Haxe models and exhaustive closed variants, forbids Reflect, Dynamic, Any, untyped, open maps, and unchecked casts as model substitutes, and confines inherently untyped external APIs to validated typed adapters with malformed-input tests.

close_reason: Completed E2.T01 and HXC-SEM-001/003/005. Added accepted ADR 0008 plus the schema-validated primitive semantics contract; typed Haxe primitive mapping and HxcIR schema 2 conversion/nullability forms; deterministic portable/metal representation rules; strict tests for widening, narrowing, signed and unsigned behavior, NaN, infinities, negative zero, overflow, and nullable values. The compiler slice uses the Haxe type system, explicitly rejects source Dynamic, contains no Any, Reflect, or untyped escape, emits no primitive hxrt dependency, and never guesses ABI widths or uses host-dependent long. Production lowering remains honestly fail-closed at HXC1000. npm test, pre-commit, snapshots, Beads lint/cycle checks, and GitHub Governance run 29444783049 including required GCC and Clang lanes all passed.

CDeclarationPlanner now consumes TypedCContractSnapshot schema 2 with merged build-fact ownerModulePaths, computes deterministic public/private header and provisional source placement, preserves include reasons, orders complete definitions, uses typed forwards for pointer cycles, propagates authoritative opaque-import includes, derives portable path guards, and fails invalid complete graphs or placements with actionable HXC5002. test/declaration_plan proves reversed-input byte determinism, system/local deduplication and order, same-header topological order, pointer-only cycles, public-to-private forward hiding, by-value cycles, source-only complete dependencies, mixed visibility, missing forward names, include-kind conflicts, and zero hxrt. Its test-only structural adapter produces four repeated-include header goldens; every header compiles alone and the combined consumer runs. Local evidence passed: npm test; jq empty docs/specs/*.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json; bd dep cycles --json []; bd lint --json zero; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh; pre-commit including gitleaks and Apple Clang 17 strict native lanes. Schema 2 is an intentional pre-1.0 internal contract update, while E1.T04/E1.T07/E7 still own default names, production multi-file emission, C++ export policy, native layout authority, and stable public ABI.

title: [HXC-E2-T06] Lower if, short-circuit, loops, switch, and jumps
description:

PRD sections: §12.14

acceptance_criteria:
- Nested loops/switches and labeled internal jumps are tested.
- Short-circuit operands execute exactly as Haxe specifies.
- No user-observable fallthrough is introduced.
- Unreachable or unsupported control flow has stable diagnostics.
close_reason: Completed E2.T06 in commit cd6bb19. The compiler now lowers typed Haxe if expressions and statements, exact lazy Boolean operators, while and do-while loops, pinned Int range loops, Int statement and value switches, break, and continue into explicit ordered HxcIR blocks and edges before structural C emission. Switch subjects are evaluated once, every C case and default transfers explicitly, nested loop targets are lexical and deterministic, and value-switch carriers are initialized only after a structural proof that every arm assigns the same typed local. Unsupported throw and unreachable statements retain source-positioned stable diagnostics instead of being guessed or emitted as raw C. Added deterministic HxcIR/C/symbol snapshots, validator negatives, Eval differential execution, nested break and continue coverage, exact short-circuit side-effect checks, optimized native execution, profile comparison, runtime-none proof, fixture taxonomy records, and updated architecture and lowering documentation. Evidence: npm test; scripts/hooks/pre-commit; npm run snapshots:check; bd dep cycles --json; bd lint --json. GitHub Governance run 29467351019 passed pinned-toolchain, license and provenance, required GCC, and required Clang, including generated control-flow execution at O0 and O2. Compatibility: portable and metal emit the same control-flow semantics; selected auto, minimal, and explicit none paths remain runtime-free with no hxrt include, source, feature, or symbol; no public C ABI was added or stabilized. Pattern, enum, String, Float, and fallthrough switches, arbitrary iterators, cleanup-bearing exits, and exception lowering remain deliberately fail-closed for their owning future work.

title: [HXC-E1] Reflaxe compiler core and C AST
description:

Scope: Coordinate the acceptance-tested child issues for this milestone. Parent-child hierarchy is structural and does not imply blocking.

Execution: Children may proceed in parallel unless an explicit blocking edge says otherwise.

## Success Criteria

- exhaustive declarator golden tests;
- AST printer output compiles under GCC/Clang;
- two builds in different absolute paths are byte-identical;
- stale generated files are safely removed.
close_reason: Completed the E1 Reflaxe compiler-core and C-AST milestone after all 10 child tasks closed with executable evidence. The typed C grammar tree and printer have exhaustive declarator and expression/statement goldens; the reusable direct-AST corpus compiles, links, and runs under required strict GCC and Clang lanes; cold renders and project emission are byte-identical across unrelated absolute paths and input orders; and Reflaxe-owned output tests safely remove stale generated files while preserving user-owned files. GitHub Governance run 29543742287 passed the pinned toolchain, formatter, full-history secret scan, license/provenance, both build-adapter families, and both native compiler families. This closes the deterministic AST/IR/output foundation only: general Haxe-to-C semantic lowering remains fail-closed at HXC1000, direct-AST fixtures select no hxrt, and no public ABI or broad platform-support claim is implied.

title: [HXC-E0-T01] Ratify product contracts and open ADR decisions
description:

PRD sections: §8, §9, §28

Scope: Resolve the owner decisions that affect public semantics before implementation hardens accidental choices.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

- Update affected ADRs and PRD sections without contradictory language.
- Create Beads discoveries for deferred experiments; do not hide undecided semantics in code.
notes:
Owner decision recorded 2026-07-15: repository license is GPL-3.0-only. E0.T02 still owns canonical notices/provenance and release-artifact enforcement; other E0.T01 contract decisions remain open.
Owner decision recorded 2026-07-14 in ADR 0001: direct idiomatic C and program-local specialization precede selective hxrt in every profile; portable defaults to auto plus summary, metal to minimal plus warn, explicit none proves total runtime absence. Owner decision recorded in ADR 0002: C headers and native constructs are authored Haxe-first through typed c.* and validated metadata/macros; narrow DSLs need an admission rationale and raw C remains explicit. Detailed c.* scaffold restoration is tracked by discovered issue haxe_c-od2.3; other E0.T01 owner decisions remain open.
Owner decisions recorded 2026-07-14: ADR 0004 accepts immutable valid UTF-8 String storage with Unicode-scalar indexing, no normalization, deterministic U+FFFD replacement for malformed decoding, and separate Bytes/CString/ABI views. ADR 0005 accepts a selective precise non-moving stop-the-world mark-and-sweep backend with exact compiler-emitted roots and explicit interior/foreign-pointer rules. ADR 0006 accepts explicit HxcIR failure/cleanup edges, proven result lowering first, otherwise a contained strict-C11 frame/cleanup slice; no transfer crosses public/foreign/callback/thread boundaries. ADR 0007 accepts strict ISO C11/no extensions, c_output plus c/target.name=c identity, target.unicode without target.utf16, and the exact 1.0 platform lanes. Deferred target-identity probes are discovery haxe_c-od2.4; implementation experiments remain with E4.T03, E4.T06, E4.T09, and E10.T01.
close_reason: Acceptance complete for HXC-PROD-002, HXC-PROD-004, HXC-PROD-005, and HXC-SCAF-006. GPL-3.0-only is recorded in LICENSE/README/PRD. Accepted ADRs: 0001 direct C/selective runtime, 0002 Haxe-first typed C authoring, 0004 valid UTF-8 scalar-indexed strings, 0005 precise non-moving collector, 0006 explicit failure edges and contained C11 unwinding, and 0007 strict C11 target identity plus 1.0 platform tuples. PRD, architecture, configuration, README, AGENTS, ADR index, research notes, and Beads seed references agree. Deferred work is explicit in discoveries haxe_c-od2.3 and haxe_c-od2.4 and owning tasks E4.T03/E4.T06/E4.T09/E10.T01. Evidence: Haxe 4.3.7 --interp string probe produced astral length/code 1/128512, composed/decomposed 1/2, embedded NUL 3; jq empty docs/specs/beads-plan.json docs/specs/diagnostics.json docs/specs/stdlib-ledger.json; cc -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic -fsyntax-only public runtime header; c++ -std=c++17 equivalent public-header gate; bd dep cycles --json returned []; bd lint --json returned zero issues; git diff --check passed.

notes:
ADR 0007 is now normative for bootstrap identity: c_output=<directory> is the reliable Haxe 4/Reflaxe activation signal; expose c/target.name=c, treat reflaxe_c as implementation-owned, enable target.unicode without target.utf16, and derive other platform defines from capabilities. Discovery haxe_c-od2.4 depends on this pin and owns the conflict/stdlib-branch probes.
E0.T02 added docs/specs/third-party-provenance.json. When selecting the actual Reflaxe resolution, replace the pin-pending status with the immutable sourceRevision/version and update its immutable license evidence URL. If resolution vendors any source, set bundled=true, declare exact paths, retain a hashed local license, and keep scripts/ci/check_license_policy.py green.
Evidence clarification: the selected upstream Reflaxe tree contains pre-existing trailing whitespace, so a raw git diff --check over newly added vendor bytes reports upstream formatting. The repository-owned whitespace gate is bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh; immutable third-party paths are excluded from formatting/whitespace rewriting and are instead protected byte-for-byte by the toolchain bundle checksum and upstream diff proof. That scoped whitespace gate passes.
close_reason: Acceptance complete for HXC-SCAF-002, HXC-SCAF-006, and HXC-SCAF-007. Haxe 4.3.7 at e0b355c6be312c1b17382603f018cf52522ec651, Reflaxe 4.0.0-beta-compatible commit 73a983112e039daad46b37912ab238df6bf0cf53/tree 05ab4abc670b6e3ed5424e78c622b92979b5affb, its 62-file SHA-256 bundle, and Lix 17.0.2 npm integrity are locked by .haxerc, package-lock.json, docs/specs/toolchain-lock.json, and provenance policy. Compiler-only Reflaxe is vendored with its MIT license and is not hxrt. npm test passed exact-tool verification, cold source probes, reversed-order negative diagnostics, duplicate idempotence, back-to-back C/non-C/C compiler-server isolation, an unrelated-CWD staged-package mount, package notices, license policy, and 8 governance tests. jq empty docs/specs/*.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json passed; bd dep cycles --json returned []; bd lint --json returned zero issues; git diff --check passed; diff -qr/q proved vendor/reflaxe/{src,LICENSE,haxelib.json} matches the selected upstream checkout. docs/toolchain.md, README, PRD, research notes, AGENTS, CI, and pre-commit policy document and enforce the pin/update and bootstrap-before-init contracts. Full adapter typing/HXC1000 remains E0.T04; target.name/stdlib conflict coverage remains haxe_c-od2.4.

title: [HXC-E0-T02] Choose license and complete third-party notices
description:
Stable plan key: E0.T02
Requirement IDs: HXC-SCAF-001, HXC-SCAF-006, HXC-QA-011, HXC-QA-013
PRD sections: §22, §28

Context: Replace the scaffold license placeholder with an approved repository license and auditable notices.

Scope: Replace the scaffold license placeholder with an approved repository license and auditable notices.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Dependencies: E0.T01

Validation: Satisfy every acceptance item with executable evidence and the applicable PRD §40 definition-of-done gates; record exact commands and artifacts in the close reason.

Compatibility: Assess and document portable/metal, runtime-policy, environment, generated-C, and public-ABI impact where applicable. Do not harden an unresolved public contract without an accepted ADR.

Documentation: Update affected ADRs, documentation, diagnostics, capability data, and ledgers required by the acceptance criteria and PRD §40.
acceptance_criteria:
- Canonical license text is checked in.
- Reflaxe, Haxe-derived standard-library work, Clang tooling, and runtime dependencies have attribution/provenance entries.
- CI rejects missing license/notice files in release artifacts.
notes:
Owner selected GPL-3.0-only on 2026-07-15 and authorized checking in the canonical text during repository preparation. This task still owns third-party notices/provenance and CI/release-artifact enforcement.
Implemented an auditable distribution inventory and fail-closed gate: THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md; LICENSES/HAXE-STDLIB-MIT.txt; docs/specs/third-party-provenance.json; scripts/ci/check_license_policy.py; governance CI; seven source/package negative and positive tests; pre-commit integration; and contributor/PRD/README rules. External development tools are distinguished from redistributed source. Unowned vendor/runtime files and unlisted std/c/_std files fail. Generated-output/runtime redistribution treatment was discovered as haxe_c-od2.5 and now blocks E8.T10 rather than being guessed here.
Post-close hardening added an eighth negative test and a validator rule requiring every component-local license (and every derived-source notice) to appear in release.requiredFiles with the same hash. Final governance suite: python3 -m unittest discover -s test/governance -p test_*.py ran 8 tests successfully.
close_reason: Satisfied HXC-SCAF-001, HXC-SCAF-006, HXC-QA-011, and HXC-QA-013. Canonical GPL-3.0-only LICENSE hash is enforced; Reflaxe, Haxe compiler/stdlib, LLVM/Clang, and the explicit empty third-party runtime inventory are recorded at immutable evidence revisions in THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md and docs/specs/third-party-provenance.json; the Haxe stdlib MIT notice is retained under LICENSES/. scripts/ci/check_license_policy.py rejects unsafe paths, unowned vendor/runtime files, unlisted or unhashed std/c/_std work, missing/modified source notices, and missing/modified release-package notices. .github/workflows/governance.yml, pre-commit, README, PRD, and AGENTS rules are wired. Validation passed: jq empty docs/specs/beads-plan.json docs/specs/diagnostics.json docs/specs/stdlib-ledger.json docs/specs/third-party-provenance.json; python3 scripts/ci/check_license_policy.py; python3 -m unittest discover -s test/governance -p test_*.py (7 tests); an assembled-tree python3 scripts/ci/check_license_policy.py --package-root smoke; strict C11/C++17 public-header parses; bash -n scripts/hooks/pre-commit; workflow YAML parse; bd dep cycles --json ([]); bd lint --json (0); git diff --check. Governance-only change: no generated-C, profile/runtime selection, environment, or public-ABI behavior changed. Generated-output/runtime redistribution terms are intentionally owned by discovered decision haxe_c-od2.5, which blocks E8.T10.

title: [HXC-E1-T06] Build typed-AST collection and normalization adapter
description:
Stable plan key: E1.T06
Requirement IDs: HXC-SCAF-002, HXC-SCAF-003, HXC-COMP-001
PRD sections: §10.3, §10.4

Context: Collect all relevant Reflaxe module types into per-build normalized input while preserving source metadata and ownership.

Scope: Collect all relevant Reflaxe module types into per-build normalized input while preserving source metadata and ownership.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Dependencies: E1.T05, E0.T09

Validation: Satisfy every acceptance item with executable evidence and the applicable PRD §40 definition-of-done gates; record exact commands and artifacts in the close reason.

Compatibility: Assess and document portable/metal, runtime-policy, environment, generated-C, and public-ABI impact where applicable. Do not harden an unresolved public contract without an accepted ADR.

Documentation: Update affected ADRs, documentation, diagnostics, capability data, and ledgers required by the acceptance criteria and PRD §40.
acceptance_criteria:
- Compiler-server builds reset all mutable state.
- Externs, abstracts, typedefs, metadata, entry point, and module ownership are classified.
- Input order changes do not change normalized output.
- A typed AST inventory report is available for unsupported-node planning.
close_reason: Completed HXC-SCAF-002, HXC-SCAF-003, and HXC-COMP-001. CReflaxeCompiler now captures the complete typed ModuleType set at filterTypes before Reflaxe callback filtering, hands request-local pending/current arrays into a fresh CompilationContext, clears them before failure, normalizes getMainModule/getMainExpr entry facts, and installs exactly one TypedProgramInput for CCompiler. TypedAstNormalizer classifies primary and secondary module ownership, classes, interfaces, externs, enums, typedefs, abstracts and implementation classes, metadata, fields, types, expression roots, and the entry point while retaining raw compiler objects; unordered module/declaration sets are canonicalized and source-semantic field, enum, metadata, and expression-root order is retained with ordinals. TypedAstInventory emits the implementation-only HXC_TYPED_AST_INVENTORY report with path-stable owned records and declaration/class/field/expression/type/metadata coverage. The exact Reflaxe compiler-server build-cache hook is excluded from only the normalized metadata view, preventing cold/cache drift without mutating raw input. test/typed_ast proves forward/reversed module order byte identity, repeated cold determinism, rich-to-small compiler-server isolation against a cold build, all required declaration families, logical path ownership, report snapshot ownership, source-anchored HXC1000, and no generated artifact. Artifacts: src/reflaxe/c/frontend, updated adapter/context/compiler, docs/typed-ast-input.md, architecture/PRD/README/AGENTS/testing updates, test/typed_ast fixtures and reviewed JSON, central snapshot/npm/toolchain/CI/pre-commit wiring. Evidence passed: npm test; npm run test:typed-ast; npm run test:all-sources; npm run snapshots:check; python3 scripts/ci/check_toolchain.py --root .; python3 scripts/ci/check_fixture_policy.py; python3 scripts/ci/check_ci_policy.py; jq empty docs/specs/*.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json; bd dep cycles --json; bd lint --json; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh; staged local-path and Gitleaks guards; tracked pre-commit chain. GitHub Governance run https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29415928936 passed pinned-toolchain, required GCC, required Clang, and license/provenance for commit 8ab340e. Compatibility: one shared frontend for portable and metal; no runtime feature selection, environment policy, generated-C semantics, output artifact, or public ABI change. Haxe-to-HxcIR/C lowering remains deliberately fail-closed at HXC1000. Missing documented Repomix evidence was separately recorded as nonblocking discovery haxe_c-od2.8.

title: Preserve collection bounds proof across assignment RHS control flow
description: Caxecraft exposed a general lowering defect: a mutable Span index on the left of an assignment is checked before the right-hand side, but a conditional right-hand side moves the eventual store into a join block. The HxcIR validator then cannot see the bounds policy and rejects valid ordinary Haxe. Fix the owning lowering/IR contract rather than restoring an inline or static-class workaround.
design: Keep Haxe left-to-right evaluation and fail-closed bounds behavior. Carry or re-establish a typed bounds proof for the exact collection and index at the post-flow access; do not weaken HxcIR validation. Cover module-level functions and the equivalent class/function shape with a small span-lowering fixture.
acceptance_criteria: A focused span fixture compiles a non-inline function that assigns span[index] = (condition ? a : b), generated C retains a checked access in valid order, strict native output is correct in both branches, existing negative bounds diagnostics remain unchanged, the full span-lowering suite passes, and the Caxecraft water differential/sanitizer lane passes without an application workaround.
notes: Implemented CFG dominance for exact fixed-array/span bounds proofs. A proof may cross into a later basic block only when its complete producer block dominates every path to the access; proof blocks with mid-block failure jumps remain local.
