Evidence: core-binding verify plus 13 focused tests; 29-test full raylib provisioning and ABI lane; caxecraft asset, domain, playable snapshot, pilot-contract, typed-boundary, format, snapshot-catalog, and CI-policy gates; real full-inventory-gift pilot presented a validated 1280x720 framebuffer and exited within 15 seconds. Generated C has one direct billboard call, five balanced texture owners, no hxrt/allocation/goto, and release snapshots.
close_reason: Nia and Mossling now render from the reviewed entity atlas through one precise typed raylib borrow, with complete resource, generated-C, snapshot, domain, ABI, and real-frame evidence.

title: Preserve a mined block when the Adventure inventory is full
description: The playable Adventure path currently removes an eligible world block before it asks how much of that block the fixed inventory can accept. At a full stack, the later collect operation accepts zero, so the mined block disappears. This contradicts the lossless capacity rule already used for Mossling drops and Nia's gift. Keep the shared world and inventory transition target-neutral and do not patch the Raylib adapter with copied rules.
acceptance_criteria: Before mutating the world, compute whether the selected mined block can be accepted, or express one atomic typed transition that returns the exact world and inventory outcome. A full inventory leaves the block present and shows bounded capacity feedback; available capacity removes the block and adds exactly one item. Add renderer-free full/available boundary tests, a named native pilot or existing pilot extension proving visible behavior, Eval-versus-C differential coverage, and retain no-goto/no-allocation/no-hxrt generated-C evidence. Creative removal remains intentionally unlimited and must not regress.
notes: Implemented as the target-neutral Mining.attempt transition. It checks the matching stack before World.remove, returns closed BlockUnavailable/InventoryFull/Collected outcomes, preserves full-stack blocks, and leaves Creative's direct unlimited removal unchanged. GameplayProbe covers available/full/bedrock boundaries under Eval. The dedicated FullInventoryMining native Raylib pilot fills all block stacks, performs the ordinary movement/mining path, presents a bounded BLOCK STACK FULL message, captures the retained target, and exits automatically. The playable gate now snapshots Mining.h/MiningResult.h/Mining.c and asserts generated C orders acceptedAmount before World.remove before collectBlock in split/package/unity, while rejecting goto, allocation, and hxrt. The full Caxecraft reference lane passed exact Eval-versus-split/package/unity traces and native O2 differential runs. Visual inspection also caught and fixed the earlier berry-specific message on a rejected block; English and Spanish now distinguish berry versus block capacity.
close_reason: Lossless Adventure mining, precise bilingual capacity feedback, focused compiler regression, native visual pilot, all-layout generated-C policy, and Eval/C differential evidence all pass.

title: Keep decoded Beads history scanning compatible with migrated NULL fields
description: After the reviewed Beads 1.1.0 migration, bd history --json fails for older issue revisions whose description column is SQL NULL. The guarded push correctly stops after Gitleaks reports no findings, because pipefail sees the history failure; it therefore cannot publish new issue data. Current records remain readable and histories created with non-null fields are readable.
design: Keep the publication guard fail closed. Do not skip unreadable revisions, squash Dolt history, bypass Gitleaks, or accept opaque Git scanning as decoded-history evidence. Prefer an upstream-compatible history reader that treats nullable historical text fields as nullable, or a reviewed query path that decodes every historical row. Preserve the exact reviewed-client contract and add a regression using a migrated NULL historical field.
acceptance_criteria: npm run security:beads-history scans current records and every historical issue revision after migration; a fixture with a NULL historical description is decoded and scanned; any history/query/decode error stops before bd dolt push; npm run beads:push reaches the push step only after the decoded scan passes; security-tooling and governance checks pass; the workaround and any upstream dependency are documented.
notes: 2026-07-21 module-fields handoff: the decoded scan now reports all 244 current issue IDs and Gitleaks reports no findings, but scripts/security/run-beads-gitleaks.sh still exits 1 before its explicit OK line, so push-safe stops before the Dolt push phase. Reproduced directly: the scanner prints no leaks and returns scan-exit=1. The guard was not bypassed; new haxe_c-xge.19.9 and closed haxe_c-xge.36 remain local until this owner is fixed.

title: Lower short-circuit conditions with helper calls without an uninitialized temporary
description: Caxecraft Mossling.step exposed HXC1001 TVar(tmp:uninitialized) for `surface < 0 || absolute(candidateY - state.y) > 1.0`. Splitting the two early-return conditions is clear and preserves behavior, but ordinary Haxe short-circuit expressions must carry every intermediate definition and evaluation edge through HxcIR. Reduce the exact Boolean shape before generalizing.
acceptance_criteria: Add a minimal fixture covering || and && with a pure helper call on the right, including a branch where the right side must not execute. Lower without an uninitialized carrier, preserve left-to-right short-circuit behavior in generated C, and cover runtime side-effect order, strict native compilation, deterministic split/package/unity output, and no goto/allocation/runtime dependency for the direct scalar case.
notes: Implemented a fail-closed flow-carrier rule for Haxe/Reflaxe-generated uninitialized locals immediately followed by an exhaustive assigning if or switch. Added FlowCarrierFixture with record-shaped ||/&& chains, skipped/evaluated helper paths, isolated deterministic split/package/unity production builds, strict Clang O0/O2 execution (GCC unavailable on this host), and explicit no-goto/no-allocation/no-hxrt checks. The Caxecraft combat pilot and full domain layout/native matrix pass with the original ordinary Haxe short-circuit expression.
close_reason: The compiler now lowers exhaustive if/switch-assigned flow carriers safely, and the focused runtime, generated-C, layout-determinism, and strict-native regressions pass.

title: Select a C representation for enum values stored in records
description: Caxecraft exposed a general lowering gap with an ordinary closed Haxe enum stored in a direct record. Passing GuideState to a function reaches HXC1001 at the phase field: the enum value awaits its representation-selection pass. The equivalent small enum abstract works because its integer carrier is already explicit, but ordinary enums in aggregates should not fail merely because the record crosses a function boundary. Reduce this to a compiler fixture and identify whether representation selection, aggregate planning, or function-signature lowering loses the field decision.
acceptance_criteria: Add a minimal ordinary-Haxe fixture with an enum-valued direct record passed to and read by a function. Select and preserve one valid C representation through HxcIR and CAST; reject unsupported payload shapes with a precise diagnostic rather than failing after partial lowering. Cover runtime semantics, split/package/unity generated shape, strict native compilation, determinism, and no goto/allocation/runtime dependency when the enum is nullary and directly representable. Re-run the Caxecraft actor slice using an ordinary enum only if that source form remains the clearest model.
notes:
The first reduction also proved that replacing the enum field with an enum-abstract field does not solve the record boundary: HXC1001 reports that the abstract is not an admitted primitive/native pointer contract. Caxecraft temporarily stores a validated Int carrier behind GuideNpc.phase/Mossling.mode, with the compromise named in source. Acceptance must cover both ordinary enum and nominal enum-abstract record fields.
The paced-combat snapshot adds concrete output-quality pressure: repeated MosslingMode.fromCode calls expand into nested C if chains (for example the simple wind-up draw predicate and attacksThisTick), even though the output remains correct, goto-free, allocation-free, and runtime-free. Restoring a nominal enum/enum-abstract record field under this issue should remove the temporary Int carrier and its repeated validation without making Caxecraft use raw numeric mode tests.
Partial general compiler capability completed while fixing haxe_c-xge.17.1: closed-record structural identity now unfolds any admitted non-core Haxe abstract to the same underlying representation used by field planning. A focused SwitchState enum-abstract record fixture proves named Haxe field semantics, direct int32 HxcIR/C storage, deterministic snapshots, strict native execution, and zero runtime. Caxecraft now uses MiningOutcome directly in MiningResult rather than an Int carrier, and its all-layout differential lane passes. This resolves the enum-abstract half only. Ordinary Haxe enum fields and migration of GuideState/MosslingState remain open under this issue and are still required before closure.

title: Preserve aggregate field values when later fields create control flow
description: Caxecraft inventory construction exposed HXC9000 when an ordinary immutable record literal evaluated several fields with an inline clamping helper. Each helper contains branches. A value produced for an earlier field was later referenced by the final aggregate-construction instruction from a block where that value was unavailable. The same source is valid Haxe and has a direct fixed-layout representation. Today the game materializes each checked scalar in a named local before constructing the record; that keeps the source readable and emits direct allocation-free C, but the compiler should make ordinary left-to-right aggregate construction safe without this scheduling constraint.
design: Apply the existing evaluation-order stabilization rule to aggregate and imported-struct field construction. When a later field expression can create control flow, preserve earlier field values in typed flow locals and reload them at the final construction point, or use an equivalent verified HxcIR schedule. Do the same for any multi-value builder with this hazard rather than adding a Caxecraft special case. Keep validation fail-closed and preserve exact source field evaluation order, aggregate identity, generated-C readability, empty hxrt, and allocation-free output.
acceptance_criteria: A minimized ordinary-Haxe fixture reproduces the old HXC9000 with a direct record literal whose two or more fields call branching inline helpers; valid lowering preserves every earlier field value across later control flow and passes HxcIR validation; side-effect order matches Eval exactly; an analogous imported-struct case is audited and tested or explicitly shown not to share the path; negative validator evidence still rejects an unavailable value; generated strict C is warning-clean, allocation-free, deterministic, and structurally readable; Caxecraft can remove the temporary named-scalar materialization while its inventory and graphical pilots continue to pass.
notes:
Refined after production compilation on 2026-07-21: making the eight checked field values explicit before the record literal removed HXC9000. Keeping Inventory methods as normal calls then restored Caxecraft compile time from roughly 40 seconds under broad inlining to about 12 seconds. The issue is field-evaluation stabilization, not generic loop-carried record storage.
Caxecraft combat added a second exact reproducer: Mossling.strike constructed a record from several state projections while a later inline mode(state) argument created conditional control flow. HxcIR validation reported an earlier field value unavailable at the conditional join. Evaluating the branching mode first makes the valid source compile, but haxe_c-940 still owns the general left-to-right stabilization. The production HXC9000 bridge now retains the validator's stable IR instruction path and source span, which identified this owner immediately instead of highlighting only the entire Main function.

title: Admit an ownership-explicit RaylibHx texture slice
description: Caxecraft already has reviewed original PNG assets, but the current RaylibHx core intentionally omits every GPU-resource API. Add the smallest reusable raw texture slice needed to load, validate, draw, and unload a Texture2D. Preserve exact raylib 6.0 ABI facts and keep ownership visible: loads return one native owner, copies do not duplicate ownership, unload happens exactly once on the render thread, and no finalizer or hxrt feature is introduced. The curated semantic facade remains omitted until haxe.c can prove cleanup on every admitted exit edge; Caxecraft must isolate raw ownership inside one typed adapter rather than spread raw calls through gameplay.
design: Mechanically extend the locked raw selection with Texture2D, Rectangle, LoadTexture, IsTextureValid, DrawTexturePro, and UnloadTexture. Record ownership/lifetime/thread facts in the reviewed inventory and docs. Add native ABI/signature probes, negative/structural checks, generated-C inspection, explicit application cleanup, and graphical smoke evidence. Do not add Caxecraft compiler special cases, finalizers, hidden registries, raw C injection, or claim complete resource coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Pinned selection and lock regenerate deterministically; exact Texture2D/Rectangle layout and function signatures compile against raylib 6.0 on admitted platforms; raw API documentation names owner, copy, validity, unload, and render-thread rules; semantic facade continues to fail closed for owned loads until cleanup support exists; Caxecraft loads checked-in assets through one typed adapter, validates every load, draws at least the title/terrain/HUD slice, unloads each owner exactly once before CloseWindow, retains an empty hxrt plan, and passes generated-C snapshots plus real-Raylib smoke/resource checks.
close_reason: Completed the ownership-explicit RaylibHx texture slice. The schema-2 selection/lock now pins Rectangle, Texture/Texture2D, LoadTexture, IsTextureValid, DrawTexturePro, and UnloadTexture with one-owner/copy/borrow/render-thread rules; generated raw declarations and the independent ABI probe cover 9 records, 2 aliases, and 59 functions. Caxecraft owns four reviewed textures in one typed adapter, stages exact hash-verified title/wordmark/HUD/item bytes, checks every load, draws the title and hotbar, and unloads each valid owner once before CloseWindow. The semantic owning load remains fail-closed until cleanup lowering exists. Verified with test_core_binding.py, test_provision.py, test:raylib-provisioning, test:caxecraft-assets, registered snapshot check/update, test:caxecraft-playable, and the real-Raylib move-jump-edit graphical pilot; generated output retains empty hxrt, no allocation calls, no goto, exact resource-call counts, and bounded automatic exit.

title: Admit typed static borrowed pointer forwarding for C imports
description: Caxecraft macOS window startup needs to pass Core Foundation kCFRunLoopDefaultMode, a header-owned static borrowed pointer, directly to CFRunLoopRunInMode. The current direct-import slice correctly rejects all non-CString pointer parameters because retention and lifetime are not modeled. Add the smallest general compiler contract that can prove this exact non-retaining forwarding without weakening the default pointer rejection or lying about the HxcIR type. Replace the temporary header-only Caxecraft platform shim once this capability is native.
design: Model header-owned static pointer constants separately from arbitrary pointer values. A call parameter must carry an explicit borrowed/non-retaining contract and compatible const/nullability facts; the imported constant supplies static lifetime provenance. Preserve the pointer in HxcIR, validate producer-to-call lifetime compatibility, and emit the header expression directly. Do not generally admit c.ConstPtr parameters, infer retention from naming, allow pointer arithmetic/dereference, or represent the pointer as an integer/enum. Clang/native compilation remains ABI authority.
acceptance_criteria:
- A typed Haxe extern can declare a header-owned static const pointer constant and pass it only to a parameter explicitly declared non-retaining/borrowed.
- HxcIR records pointer type, const/nullability, static provenance, and call-consumption policy; malformed or unproven lifetime combinations fail with source-positioned diagnostics.
- Native positive evidence calls CFRunLoopRunInMode or an equivalent independent fixture directly with no raw C, cast, hxrt, or wrapper symbol.
- Negative fixtures retain rejection for arbitrary pointer parameters/returns, runtime pointers, retained/unknown APIs, mutable/nullable mismatches, dereference, and escapes.
- Generated C is strict C11, deterministic, warning-clean, and uses the authoritative header spelling/prototype.
- Caxecraft removes its temporary header-only macOS shim and its generated C calls CFRunLoopRunInMode directly.

title: Add a deterministic Caxecraft test-pilot and visual E2E interface
description:
Turn graphical Caxecraft QA into a supported interface rather than ad-hoc OS automation. The interface must let CI and an engineering agent launch the generated native game with a fixed clock, feed a typed script of player actions, observe structured gameplay/render evidence, capture reviewable frames, and exit without human input.

Start from the bounded three-frame smoke added after the first-frame renderer stall. Evolve it into deterministic action scripts for look, movement, jump, mine, place, pause/recapture, and later inventory/dialog/editor interactions. Keep ordinary interactive input unchanged and keep gameplay rules in target-neutral domain code; the test pilot is an input/clock/observation adapter, not a second game implementation.

A local agent-facing command should build or reuse the exact generated executable, run a named script, return normalized evidence paths and state, and make screenshots easy to inspect. Hosted CI should use a virtual display or the accepted deterministic software-render path with bounded timeouts. OS-level desktop automation may complement this interface to check focus, resizing, and presentation, but must not be the deterministic semantic authority.
design: Use the smallest typed seam: one closed action vocabulary, a fixed-tick scripted input source, and an observation sink owned by the application adapter. Prefer a versioned JSON scenario/result boundary only at the Python runner, validated immediately into typed Haxe data or compiled named fixtures until general file parsing is supported. Record world/player hashes, selected cell, edits, completed frames/ticks, render counters, and explicit termination reason. Images must be checked structurally (dimensions, nonblank regions, stable semantic probes); exact cross-GPU pixel hashes are not portable evidence. A later software renderer may own byte-exact golden images. Never inject raw C, special-case Caxecraft in the compiler, sleep blindly, or depend on real-time keyboard/mouse timing for semantic assertions.
acceptance_criteria:
- A documented local command runs a named deterministic gameplay script through generated C and exits on its own with a bounded timeout.
- Scripts cover launch/first frame, move/collide/jump, look/select, remove/place/reject-overlap, pause/recapture, and clean quit; later gameplay systems add scripts with their features.
- Results include a schema version, script identity/input hash, fixed tick/frame counts, player/world/edit/selection evidence, render counters, exit reason, native compiler/Raylib identity, and normalized screenshot paths without checkout/temp paths.
- Repeated runs produce identical semantic evidence; malformed/unknown actions and version drift fail closed.
- CI runs the real generated native executable under a virtual display or accepted software-render backend and fails if initialization or a frame exceeds its budget.
- At least one visual assertion proves the framebuffer is nonblank and contains expected sky/terrain/HUD regions without claiming cross-driver byte identity.
- An agent guide explains how to run a script, inspect frames/evidence, add a regression, and when real desktop/manual play is still required.
- Interactive builds contain no test-pilot behavior or test-only runtime dependency, and generated output retains the normal no-raw-C, empty-hxrt, allocation, readability, and direct-Raylib gates.
notes:
2026-07-21 foundation: the automated C build now captures its second real Raylib framebuffer, validates PNG framing/checksums/filters, requires exact 1280x720 dimensions, and proves substantial exact sky and grass regions before accepting the three-frame smoke. The Linux CI desktop lane reuses its O2 compile/link as this graphical run under Xvfb, avoiding a duplicate Haxe compile. General typed action scripts, structured semantic state, presentation probing, repeated-run determinism, and the agent guide remain open under this issue.
2026-07-21 typed-pilot evidence: named launch, move-jump-edit, and pause-recapture scripts now share target-neutral typed actions and an exact fixed-tick clock. The generated native executable captures and validates its own real Raylib frame, exits within a bounded frame count, and rejects missing or malformed expected visual roles. Focused Eval probes cover exact checkpoints and C/es_MX.UTF-8 determinism. Structured semantic-state JSON, repeated-run artifact comparison, and hosted desktop coverage remain open.
2026-07-21 actor-pilot evidence: MoveJumpEdit now includes a typed Interact action and the real framebuffer validator requires Nia-coat and Mossling-crown pixels in addition to sky, terrain, HUD, and light UI. This detects a missing actor render rather than merely a nonblank window. The pilot remains bounded and ordinary builds retain no scripted-input channel.
2026-07-21 recovery-pilot evidence: RecoveryUse starts from a typed two-heart fixture fact at the existing compile-time pilot-provider boundary, selects berries, sends the same secondary intent as real right click, requires unique successful-recovery pixels after EndDrawing, and exits in four frames plus the independent 15-second timeout. The 1280x720 native framebuffer passed. Ordinary builds contain neither the fixture selection nor scripted input. Versioned semantic reports, repeated semantic-result comparison, and hosted graphical coverage remain open.
2026-07-21 capacity-pilot evidence: FullInventoryGift fills the berry stack through a typed provider fixture, performs Nia's two real interaction actions, then requires a unique full-capacity warning in the presented 1280x720 framebuffer while Nia remains on the gift dialogue. It exits in four frames. Headless gameplay proof separately covers partial drop retention.
The named pilot adapter now supplies exactly one 50 ms simulation step per rendered frame instead of Raylib wall time, while interactive builds retain measured elapsed time. CombatDrop expanded to 40 bounded frames and the screenshot gate requires exact damage-feedback plus berry-drop colors after one warned impact and three cooldown-paced strikes. Semantic report export, repeated-native report equality, and hosted graphical CI remain open acceptance.
close_reason: Delivered the deterministic native Caxecraft pilot and visual E2E interface. Named Haxe scripts drive the ordinary input/gameplay path under a fixed clock; real Raylib frames are flushed, captured, structurally validated, and bounded to self-exit. Pilot-only Haxe telemetry exports a versioned 32-word state record, the host validates it and writes a normalized JSON report, and two executions of the same native binary must match exactly. Linux CI runs the generated executable under Xvfb and preserves the report plus screenshot. Release snapshots prove the input channel, counters, strip, and explicit flush are compiled out. The framebuffer carrier is explicitly frozen as a temporary one-shot bootstrap; haxe_c-xge.19.8.1 now depends on generic Haxe I/O/JSON owners for the future all-Haxe stdin/stdout bridge, with no application C shim. Verified test:caxecraft-pilot, test:caxecraft-playable, test:caxecraft-assets, CI policy, all-sources, fixture policy, Haxe formatting, typed boundaries, real macOS launch-smoke double-run report, and visual review of its 1280x720 title frame.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Transport decision after review: the 32-word framebuffer strip is accepted only as a frozen, one-shot bootstrap for the current native graphical pilot. It is Haxe-authored, removed from release builds, uses the already admitted Raylib path, and proves the final framebuffer, but its low bandwidth and graphics coupling make it unsuitable for live control. Do not grow the word set into a console or agent protocol. Replace final telemetry with Haxe standard output, then use a bounded framed Haxe stdin/stdout pipe for live request/response when haxe_c-8al and any required structured serialization support land. A Caxecraft-owned C helper or extern shim is forbidden.

title: Migrate shared Beads schema and pin hook compatibility
description: The pre-commit hook resolves whichever bd binary appears first on PATH. This checkout's guarded remote is on Beads 1.0.4 schema v32, but system Beads 1.1.0 rewrote the passive issues.jsonl export into a different ordering/shape before refusing remote-backed schema migration. That changed the staged tree and invalidated three already-passing exact toolchain shard receipts, starting a duplicate exhaustive matrix.
design: Use this checkout as the single designated migrator from Beads 1.0.4 schema v32 to reviewed Beads 1.1.0 schema v53, preserve a full embedded-Dolt backup and semantic JSONL comparison, then immediately publish the migrated database. After publication, require repository hooks and guarded Beads tooling to resolve the reviewed 1.1.0 client before any database or passive-export operation. Other clones must adopt the remote database instead of migrating independently. Keep the passive JSONL exporter byte-stable so unchanged toolchain shard receipts remain reusable.
acceptance_criteria: The designated migration has a recoverable v32 backup; schema v53 contains the same 213 semantic issue records and has no dependency cycles; the migrated Dolt history is published once; clean 1.1.0 exports are byte-stable; an incompatible PATH-first bd cannot rewrite or stage files; other clones are told to adopt rather than independently migrate; failed shard retry reuses unchanged successful receipts and reruns only failed shards; governance tests cover version discovery, JSONL/index no-change, receipt identity, and actionable diagnostics; focused and hosted Governance pass.
notes:
Observed 2026-07-20 while committing staged tree c24ef18598945cdcc42cb391593859325734e09b. The first exhaustive hook passed contracts, lowering-objects, and lowering-semantics. System bd 1.1.0 then rewrote 394 JSONL lines on retry, so all three receipts reported changed inputs. Verified bd 1.0.4 (release SHA-256 0c53479fea070a1cabe8eb31e3824d74c5643b1deca71a5fe832ebd38e9ef877) restored the intended one-line issue export. No schema migration was run.
Maintainer explicitly selected migration to Beads 1.1.0 on 2026-07-21. This checkout is the designated single migrator. Pre-migration backup: /private/tmp/haxe-c-beads-1.1-migration.AdvanD (local recovery only), 213-issue JSONL SHA-256 d294d949dd89f79640f380d57dd673d8f1e60750bdadc609a1d17f4086878a31. BD_ALLOW_REMOTE_MIGRATE=1 bd migrate applied 21 migrations from schema v32 to v53. A normalized semantic comparison of pre/post exports matched exactly; only dependency serialization order and migration-updated timestamps changed.

title: Profile and reduce the remaining CBodyEmitter traversal cost
description: The first compiler-profile slice removed duplicate body construction, but the post-change Caxecraft profile still spends about 11.6 seconds in the one required HxcIR-to-CAST body traversal. This is now the dominant request-local compiler phase and is not improved by Haxe compilation-server reuse.
design: Instrument inside CBodyEmitter before changing algorithms. Attribute time by closed operation or function family, look for repeated immutable lookups, traversals, structural rebuilding, or accidental quadratic work, and implement only a target-earned optimization supported by the profile. Preserve HxcIR evaluation order, place/value distinctions, source mapping, non-returning-cycle specialization, readable C shaping, deterministic names, and fresh CompilationContext state. Do not cache mutable TypedExpr or compiler objects and do not add a Caxecraft-specific compiler path. Time-box this lane so it improves the flagship edit loop without delaying the next playable Caxecraft content milestone.
acceptance_criteria: Record at least three cold and three post-prime warm split Caxecraft samples with host conditions; identify the dominant CBodyEmitter subphase with executable evidence; demonstrate a meaningful reduction against the haxe_c-fbq 11.4-11.7 second body baseline or document why the bounded experiment rejects the proposed optimization; preserve byte-identical normal artifacts and source/package/unity semantics; pass body-lowering, function-lowering including recursive non-returning cycles, project-layout, Caxecraft Eval/native O2 differential, typed-boundary, determinism, and Governance gates; retain direct cold builds and safe Haxe-server invalidation.
notes: Read-only hotspot audit found a high-confidence algorithmic lead. Caxecraft DomainProbe.selfCheck has 29 explicit ifs and 30 early failure returns. CBodyControlFlow.normalJoin can test every reachable block and, per candidate, recompute forward reachability, completion fixed points, prefix walks, and BFS distances; immediate post-dominator work is also repeated by builder and validator. This can approach polynomial/cubic behavior and matches the workload. Production source-line directives are disabled and are not the likely cause. Instrument per-function control-flow subphases first, then preserve exact candidate scoring while proving ranked candidates best-first; next consider request-local immutable successor/postdominator facts and block/local/value/cleanup indexes. Add an early-return-ladder work-counter regression and require byte-identical output. A GPT-5.6 Pro review is deferred unless measured local fixes fail or incremental ownership needs a broader design decision.
close_reason: Caxecraft profiling identified control-flow planning as the body bottleneck; exact-score ranked join proof reduced the final cold control-flow median to 522ms while preserving byte-identical artifacts. All named semantic, native, determinism, snapshot, typed-boundary, and Governance gates passed.

title: Split CAXEMAP semantic validation by domain ownership
description: The exhaustive malformed-input audit grew ScenarioValidator to roughly 755 lines. Its world geometry, object/story identity, CaxeFlow, source-coordinate lookup, and diagnostic accumulation policies are coherent but no longer pleasant to review as one class. Split the implementation before adding persistence, audio, or executor semantics so Caxecraft remains readable showcase Haxe.
design: Preserve ScenarioValidator.validate as the one caller-facing operation. Introduce small package-internal, typed collaborators for shared validation context/source coordinates, world and placement checks, story checks, and CaxeFlow graph/action checks. Document @:noCompletion on internal helpers in plain language: it hides implementation-only types from editor completion and does not change runtime visibility or safety. Keep one diagnostic sink and the current deterministic validation order. This is an output-inert refactor: no accepted file, diagnostic kind, coordinate, ordering, or model trace may change.
acceptance_criteria: No validator implementation file is an undifferentiated semantic blob; each class has one plain-language responsibility; game/editor code still calls ScenarioParser.parse and cannot bypass final validation accidentally; @:noCompletion usage states exactly why it is present; the complete exact malformed-input probe, canonical bytes, model hash, typed-boundary gate, all-source gate, formatting gate, and generated-C scenario parity remain unchanged; focused test time does not materially regress.
close_reason: Completed the output-inert typed split in commit be65f8b. ScenarioValidator remains the sole public facade and makes the original feature, world, identity, object, story, flow, and extension diagnostic order explicit. ScenarioValidationContext owns one diagnostic sink, source coordinates, and shared identity indexes; the document, world, story, and CaxeFlow validators each own one domain. Every internal helper documents that @:noCompletion only hides editor suggestions and does not provide runtime privacy or safety. Canonical 1196/4025-byte output, exact malformed diagnostics and coordinates, model hash, nominal identities, generated-C/native parity, zero hxrt/allocation behavior, Haxe formatting, typed-boundary, all-source, governance, diff hygiene, and pre-commit checks passed. Remote Governance passed all jobs: https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29790071115. No behavior, output, runtime, or ABI changed; persistence, execution, and editor behavior remain deferred to their owning issues.

title: Add original music, sound effects, and typed audio authoring to Caxecraft
description: Give Caxecraft a small original soundtrack and a coherent set of sound effects, then expose them through the same typed public content path as the rest of the game. Deliver music for the menu, exploration regions, underwater route, castle/boss, and resolution plus bounded effects for UI, movement/material interaction, blocks, pickups, combat, water, bridge/suit progression, checkpoints, Ivvy, enemies, and Browser. Add game settings, lifecycle, packaging, CaxeMap/CaxeFlow references, and editor preview/selection rather than hard-coding filenames in game logic.
design: Use MIDI Standard MIDI File type 1 and a pinned human-editable composition project as source where useful, but ship streamed Ogg Vorbis for music and short PCM WAV files for effects. Raylib 6.0 directly supports WAV/OGG and streaming but not MIDI synthesis; shipping MIDI would require a synthesizer and instrument bank whose output, size, licensing, and cross-platform behavior become extra runtime policy. Pin every original source, render tool/version, instrument or synthesis recipe, loudness/export setting, license, output hash, and loop point so clean builds never depend on an online service. Extend the version-locked RaylibHx layer with explicit owned Sound/Music handles and balanced init/load/update/play/stop/unload/close behavior. Shared Haxe emits typed semantic audio requests; a narrow raylib adapter owns playback. Asset packs map ContentId values to logical paths. CaxeFlow gets only small closed actions such as play sound, select/stop music, and optional object-local positioning after their deterministic semantics are ratified. No rule contains a host path. Music, effects, ambience, and master volume are separate bounded buses. Essential cues always have visual/caption equivalents.
acceptance_criteria: A reviewed audio brief and cue sheet names each track/effect, emotional purpose, trigger, duration/loop behavior, polyphony/priority, and explicit omissions; original editable sources and deterministic offline exports produce byte-pinned OGG/WAV assets with provenance and no copied game melody or trade dress; the locked raylib raw and ergonomic layers cover the admitted audio structs/functions with native ABI probes, ownership documentation, failure handling, and zero leaks; the game initializes and closes audio safely, streams music every frame, handles pause/focus/device failure, crossfades or transitions without clicks, limits simultaneous effects, and exposes master/music/effects/ambience controls; CaxeMap asset catalogs and CaxeFlow use stable typed audio IDs, reject missing/wrong-kind assets, and serialize canonically; the editor can choose, preview, stop, and validate cues and author simple music-zone/trigger behavior with keyboard/mouse/controller support; English and es-MX captions/settings are complete and no required clue depends only on sound; Eval uses a semantic no-device oracle while generated C native tests prove real raylib calls, deterministic event traces, cleanup, packaging, strict GCC/Clang O0/O2, sanitizers, allocation/runtime-feature budgets, and representative generated-C readability.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Audio-direction clarification from the maintained Caxecraft policy: use block-building adventure and playful fantasy RPG moods only as broad genre references; all melodies, arrangements, sound designs, characters, and trade dress remain original. The bounded cue sheet must explicitly cover title/menu, village safety, exploration, mystery or puzzles, underwater travel, snow, ash, castle tension, Browser boss battle, victory, and game over, with transitions that follow gameplay mood. The effects sheet should cover UI, material footsteps, blocks, inventory/pickups, water, creatures, Ivvy, combat, mechanisms, and story events. Editable MIDI or other pinned sources remain production inputs; the runtime receives reproducibly rendered OGG music and PCM WAV effects through stable content IDs.

title: Implement versioned Caxecraft world-state and save-game persistence
description: Add real save/load for live Caxecraft sessions. Keep authored maps and player progress separate: a .caxemap remains the validated source world, while a versioned save records the changing state needed to resume Creative or Adventure play without modifying that source file. The save model must cover the active map identity and compatible content fingerprint, simulation tick, Haxirio transform/health/inventory/equipment and underwater suit, modified voxel cells, object spawn/despawn/state, checkpoint, objectives/journal/routes/puzzles, CaxeFlow variables and scheduled work, deterministic choice/random state, Ivvy, NPC, enemy, and boss state where required for exact recovery.
design: Define a target-neutral typed save snapshot and a small canonical bounded codec before filesystem work. Load map first, verify the save version/map identity/content compatibility, decode and validate a complete candidate snapshot, then replace live simulation state only on success. Use stable IDs and explicit typed variants; never persist pointers, translated strings, absolute paths, process data, or wall-clock ordering. Adventure progress should normally be an overlay on the immutable authored map; Creative-world ownership and editor drafts stay explicitly distinct. Reuse the atomic temporary-write/flush/replace adapter owned by haxe_c-xge.19.4, with typed migrations only when a later version has a proved conversion.
acceptance_criteria: A reviewed save-format reference distinguishes authored map, editor draft, Creative world, and Adventure progress; save/load restores every declared mutable state family byte- and trace-deterministically; corrupted, truncated, oversized, incompatible-map/content, duplicate-ID, and unsupported-version inputs fail with exact localized diagnostics and leave the running session unchanged; checkpoint retry cannot lose or duplicate quest-critical items; manual slots, autosave, and recovery retain the last known-good file under injected create/write/flush/replace failures; Eval and generated C round-trip the same fixtures and resume to identical subsequent simulation traces; strict GCC/Clang, O0/O2, sanitizers, repeated/warm runs, and allocation/runtime-feature budgets pass; no save operation mutates the source .caxemap.
notes: Water persistence seam delivered under haxe_c-xge.20.5.3 (2026-07-21): WaterSnapshot captures canonical ascending-index source/flow bytes and exact pending queue marks into caller-owned fixed buffers. Restore preflights the whole candidate, preserves terrain, clears authored water removed during play, rejects solid/malformed overlaps without mutation, copies queue marks, and WaterSimulation.restorePending derives count/next index instead of persisting scheduler internals. PlayerAquaticState already owns medium/submersion/head/breath/drowning ticks; the full CAXESAVE codec must store those validated values plus the equipped aquatic profile's stable content ID, not the generated enum ordinal. No private water file format or filesystem adapter was added.

title: Make CAXEMAP malformed-input diagnostics exhaustive and source-precise
description: Complete the parser/validator audit before the canonical codec closes. The audit found that quoted tokens can currently impersonate structural keywords, some declared uniqueness and depth rules are not enforced, role-specific references can resolve to the wrong object kind, and several semantic failures fall back to the document header instead of the offending record.
design: Keep the staged lexer -> parser -> validator boundary. Give bare grammar words one typed helper instead of scattered quoted-flag checks. Extend source subjects only where a semantic validator genuinely needs a later coordinate. Use closed diagnostic payloads for closed limit/record families rather than adding prose strings. Resolve and document the version-1 policy for object call arguments and nested seeded choices before changing their grammar. Add one focused mutation/property table so each admitted malformed family proves exact kind, line/column/record, bounded work, and no Scenario result.
acceptance_criteria: Quoted keywords, delimiters, booleans, states, and comparisons are rejected while quoted user text remains accepted; signed-32 integer boundaries and noncanonical spellings have explicit tests; duplicate features/palette codes/routes/sequence parameters and every singleton/ID/tag family fail at an actionable source coordinate; sequence immediate-call depth, recursive choice action counts, and role-specific zone/entity/checkpoint references enforce the accepted bounds; the object-argument and nested-choice grammar is explicitly resolved in docs/model/tests; all in-scope diagnostic variants have stable exact tests and no malformed case yields a plausible Scenario; canonical fixtures remain byte-identical and the focused gate remains fast.
notes: Completed the exhaustive malformed-input audit. Structural words now require bare lexer tokens; canonical signed Int32 parsing rejects +, leading zeroes, -0, and overflow; closed diagnostics cover record/limit families; duplicate and reference errors point at their offending records; role-specific entity/zone/checkpoint references, immediate sequence depth, recursive action budgets, nested choices, and the dead object-argument spelling are resolved and documented. Focused model/codec gate passed in 1.65s; typed-boundaries, all-sources, Haxe formatting, diff hygiene, and the full Eval/split/package/unity generated-C/native differential passed. The full lane took 171.14s under load ~18, so it remains a milestone gate rather than the edit loop. Follow-up haxe_c-xge.19.2.3 owns the output-inert validator split.
close_reason: Exact fail-closed diagnostics and source coordinates are covered across the admitted CAXEMAP 1 grammar; canonical codec and generated-C parity remain green.

title: Refactor CAXEMAP readers into showcase-quality idiomatic Haxe
description: Before the codec slice closes, make its Haxe implementation itself convincing showcase material for TypeScript-oriented readers. Split the first complete grammar implementation into cohesive lexical, document/story/world, CaxeFlow, and validation responsibilities; replace encoded tags and repeated token plumbing with closed named types/helpers; keep staged fail-closed results and source coordinates explicit; and add plain-language comments only where they explain a non-obvious invariant.
design: Preserve the now-executable byte and diagnostic contract while refactoring. Prefer nominal IDs, immutable records, small final classes, exhaustive enums/switches, generic typed result helpers, and named cursor/value readers. Avoid framework abstractions, inheritance, Dynamic/Any/Reflect/untyped, or clever parser combinators whose mechanics obscure the small line grammar. A TypeScript developer should understand the happy path quickly and see Haxe's stronger modeling where it prevents mistakes.
acceptance_criteria: No scenario parser/controller source file is a procedural blob; the stages and responsibilities are clear from module names and short public entry points; closed value/record concepts do not use numeric or string tags internally; canonical and full-grammar fixtures, exact malformed diagnostics/source lines, CRLF/order convergence, bounds/property probes, formatter, typed-boundary guard, and all-source gate pass byte-identically; docs explain the code-quality rule and any remaining deliberate complexity.
notes: Baseline to preserve: minimal.caxemap 1,196 bytes, codec-full.caxemap 4,025 bytes, scenario-model hash 454598688, six named negative families with exact lines, hard-bound/property probes, and test:caxecraft-scenario-model ~1.1s. Refactor must be behavior-neutral and keep the TypeScript-facing Haxe readability directive added to AGENTS.md and the game design.
close_reason: Split the 1,101-line first-pass parser into a small ScenarioParser.parse API plus documented cursor/token, document, world, story, and CaxeFlow readers. Replaced compressed placement/action arms with named helpers and marked cross-file implementation classes @:noCompletion with a concrete application-code explanation. Preserved the 1,196/4,025-byte fixtures, model hash 454598688, exact malformed diagnostics, CRLF/order convergence, and bounds. Validation: test:caxecraft-scenario-model, test:typed-boundaries, test:all-sources, format:haxe:check, test:governance, git diff --check; independent review found no semantic blocker and both low readability notes were addressed.

title: Ship the child-friendly in-game CaxeMap and CaxeFlow editor
description: Present the public editor inside Caxecraft with world painting and placement, readable icon-backed WHEN/IF/DO cards, object picking, templates, validation overlays, save/load, undo/redo, and immediate reversible test play. The same UI must remain useful to adults through an Advanced disclosure over the same data.
design: Raylib widgets adapt renderer-independent editor commands. Default cards use large targets, icons plus text, plain English/Spanish-ready message IDs, visible focus, controller/keyboard equivalents, and world picking instead of typed IDs. Advanced mode reveals nested predicates, variables, sequences, signals, and fixed-tick delays without creating a second format.
acceptance_criteria: A user can create/resize a finite world; paint, erase, fill, select, stamp, and place every required object type; author door/key, NPC dialogue, checkpoint, enemy-wave, bridge-switch, and win-condition templates; validate, save/load, undo/redo, and enter/leave test play without restart. UI states have deterministic semantic traces, bounded steady-state allocation measurements, screenshot/review evidence, keyboard/mouse/controller coverage, and plain-language en/es-MX-ready diagnostics; all shipped placement/progression data remains on the public path.
notes:
2026-07-21 UI dependency decision: use the haxe_c-xge.19.6.1 pinned RayguiHx subset for the editor and reusable panel UI if its full compatibility gates pass. The official raygui 5.0 release is header-only, immediate-mode, zlib-licensed, and intended for tools; its exact header passes a C11 syntax/implementation compile against the pinned raylib 6.0 header. Upstream documents 6.1-dev and warns that 5.0 changed result APIs, so this probe does not replace native/runtime verification or justify floating master. The ergonomic Haxe layer must supply responsive layout, typed state/results, focus/controller behavior, localization, and Caxecraft styling rather than exposing the C API throughout game code.
CaxeTest/editor integration: the editor project tree must present campaigns, levels, regions, objects, rules, localized messages, and tests coherently. It records semantic actions through the real engine driver, offers independent assertion cards, runs/steps cases, shows bounded traces/screenshots, and navigates failures to source. Typed renames update references transactionally or fail; deletion cannot silently orphan a test. This is the public authoring UI for haxe_c-xge.19.8, not a separate debug-only simulator.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: The visual editor must expose three depths over one model: Guided icon/sentence cards and templates for children; Advanced visual cards/graph for nested predicates, event context, variables, branches, sequences, and timing; and exact CaxeMap/CaxeFlow Text with syntax coloring, completion, formatting, inline diagnostics, and world-reference navigation for experts and agents. View changes parse/validate the same typed draft, never lose behavior, and never introduce a privileged scripting language.

title: Build editor command model, validation, undo, and test-play
description: Create the renderer-independent editor semantics used by both the Raylib UI and tests: bounded world resize, paint/erase/fill/select, prefab stamps, placements, dialogue/objectives/rules, validation, undo/redo, and reversible Edit-to-Test-Play snapshots.
design: Every edit is a closed typed command with an explicit inverse or bounded before-state. A draft remains separate from the last valid playable Scenario. Test play clones the validated snapshot and discards runtime changes on exit unless a future explicitly supported import command is used.
acceptance_criteria: Eval tests create a complete small scenario through public editor commands, undo and redo every command family, validate, serialize/reload, enter/leave test play without state leakage, and recover from invalid edits; history and selection have hard bounds; the semantic core imports no Raylib/C API and uses no Dynamic/raw code.
notes: Implemented the renderer-independent editor semantics: closed typed commands for bounded resize, palette/voxel edits, selections, prefab and object placement, dialogue/objective/rule changes, and last-playable recovery; exact bounded canonical undo/redo; separate draft, last-playable, and disposable test-play state; and a target-neutral public acceptance probe. Independent GPT-5-family review found and verified fixes for unsupported-version normalization and ambiguous narrator/speaker identities, then reported no blockers. Focused editor and scenario-model gates pass under C/es_MX.UTF-8; typed boundaries report 456 files and zero new seams; all 114 Governance tests pass; the exact six-command Caxecraft shard passed in 99.796s with locale/server determinism, split/package/unity O0/O2/ASan+UBSan native parity, maintainability checks, and snapshot ownership. Native atomic persistence and the visual Raylib editor remain explicitly owned by haxe_c-xge.19.4 and haxe_c-xge.19.6.
close_reason: Renderer-independent editing, exact bounded history, validation/recovery, and isolated test play satisfy the public command, target-neutrality, determinism, native flagship, and independent-review gates.

title: Add atomic CaxeMap persistence and native parity
description: Connect the target-neutral codec to ordinary Haxe byte/text and filesystem APIs, then prove the same load/save/rule traces through generated C. Save through a validated sibling temporary and replace atomically where the platform contract supports it; failures retain the last known-good file.
design: This slice consumes general E5 String/Unicode, collections, Bytes/core I/O, and filesystem adapters rather than adding Caxecraft-only native calls. Keep user-content paths logical and sandboxed. Separate platform file operations from pure codec semantics and expose typed failure results suitable for localization.
acceptance_criteria: Eval and generated C produce identical canonical bytes and semantic traces; strict GCC/Clang, O0/O2, sanitizers, malformed UTF-8, permission/write/rename failure, cleanup, interrupted-write simulation, CRLF, locale, and deterministic warm rebuilds pass; no partial file replaces a good save and no absolute host path enters content or reports; runtime features and allocations are explicit and measured.

title: Implement deterministic CaxeFlow rule execution
description: Execute the closed WHEN/IF/DO model over fixed-tick Scenario state. Cover the version-1 events, composable predicates, ordered actions, reusable sequences, scheduling, and deferred signals required by the Adventure and child-friendly templates.
design: Rules observe one stable tick snapshot and run by priority then stable ID. Action order is source order. Signals and delayed work enter bounded deferred queues, never re-enter the current stack. Closed budgets bound events, calls, spawned entities, scheduled actions, and predicate depth. Budget exhaustion is a visible diagnostic with deterministic partial-state policy.
acceptance_criteria: Eval traces prove every version-1 event/predicate/action, priority and stable-ID order, ordered actions, once/repeat/cooldown, fixed-tick delays, reusable sequences, seeded choice, and deferred signals; adversarial cycles and budget exhaustion terminate with exact diagnostics; reversed registration and locale do not change traces; no arbitrary code, Dynamic payload, wall clock, unseeded randomness, or raw target code is admitted.
close_reason: Implemented the target-neutral deterministic CaxeFlow executor and exact Eval evidence for all 10 events, 12 predicates, 18 actions, stable order, repeat policies, sequences, scheduling, seeded choice, deferred work, cycles, and every runtime budget. Independent review blockers were fixed with signed 64-bit tick arithmetic, maximum-delay/32-bit-boundary tests, payload-complete trace hashing, exact diagnostic owners, and a tested due-batch partial-state policy. Focused CaxeFlow passed in 1.17s; exact Caxecraft model, locale/server determinism, Eval plus split/package/unity O0/O2/ASan+UBSan, and snapshot shard passed in 102.54s; all 114 Governance tests passed.

title: Implement canonical CaxeMap codec and semantic validator
description: Implement bounded scalar-UTF-8 tokenization, parsing, full-model validation, and canonical serialization for CAXEMAP 1. Loading must build and validate a candidate before replacing live state; writing the same Scenario always produces identical bytes.
design: Use a staged typed result: lexical records, parsed records, then validated Scenario. Preserve source coordinates for actionable diagnostics. Canonicalize only fields declared unordered; retain rule/action/dialogue order. Reject malformed UTF-8, bad escaping, overflow, invalid RLE totals, duplicate IDs/tags, unresolved references, impossible placements, and unsupported required features.
acceptance_criteria: Valid fixtures load and save byte-identically; CRLF input, locale, reverse discovery order, and repeated/warm Eval runs converge on canonical LF UTF-8 bytes; every malformed family has a stable typed diagnostic and never yields a plausible Scenario; fuzz/property limits prove bounded input, nesting, record, and RLE work; no JSON/Dynamic/raw injection or example-only compiler branch is used.
notes:
Foundation checkpoint implemented on 2026-07-20: bounded scalar-UTF-8 lexer, typed lexical records/coordinates, syntactic parser, compile-time content registry, semantic validator, canonical writer, optional extension retention, minimal and full-grammar byte fixtures, CRLF/input-order convergence, exact negative kinds/lines, and bounded record/text/predicate/action/UTF-8 probes. Fast Eval gate is ~1.1s. This issue remains open: the readability child haxe_c-xge.19.2.1 must split the first-pass 1,100-line document/flow reader before acceptance; additional malformed families and validation completeness remain to audit.
Final determinism gate added on 2026-07-20: two cold C-locale codec requests, one cold alternate-locale request, and two requests through one exact-pin Haxe compilation server all run the complete staged probe and require the same exact trace. The probe itself retains byte-identical minimal/full fixture round trips, repeated writes, CRLF and reverse-order convergence, exact typed malformed-input diagnostics, and bounded-work checks. Verification: focused determinism lane passed with es_MX.UTF-8 in about four seconds; fast scenario model/codec gate passed; exact 40-command toolchain policy passed; full local Governance passed with 114 tests.
close_reason: Canonical CAXEMAP codec and semantic validation now satisfy byte, diagnostic, bounded-work, locale, reverse-order, repeated, and warm-server determinism gates.

title: Ratify CAXEMAP 1 grammar and target-neutral typed model
description: Turn the accepted game-design contract into a complete, small line-oriented format and ordinary-Haxe model. Define bounds, scalar-UTF-8 escaping, canonical coordinates/order, schema evolution, object/tag identity, placements, dialogue/objectives, CaxeFlow state/rules/sequences, and closed diagnostic coordinates without Dynamic or target branches.
design: Keep syntax separate from semantics. The parser may retain source spans, but validated Scenario values use closed enums/records and logical paths. Version 1 rejects unknown required records and permits only explicitly typed optional extensions. Document technical words at first use and include child-readable examples.
acceptance_criteria: A reviewed CAXEMAP 1 reference and repository fixture define every record, field, bound, escape, ordering rule, feature-negotiation rule, and diagnostic coordinate; repository-owned Haxe types model the complete admitted structure without Dynamic/Any/Reflect/untyped/raw C; malformed and valid Eval typing fixtures cover every closed variant; docs and Beads dependencies identify the general String, collection, I/O, and filesystem capabilities needed for native C.
notes: Split haxe_c-xge.19 into six ordered children: .1 format/model, .2 codec/validator, .3 rule executor, .4 native persistence, .5 editor semantics, and .6 child-friendly Raylib UI. Native persistence explicitly depends on the general E5 String/Unicode, collection, Bytes/I/O, and filesystem owners instead of Caxecraft-only C calls. The Eval oracle types the scenario package through its HXML include boundary, so model coverage does not shift DomainProbe source maps or generated-C snapshots.
close_reason: Ratified CAXEMAP 1 as a bounded, canonical scalar-UTF-8 authoring format and added the target-neutral Haxe Scenario/CaxeFlow model. The contract defines every version-1 world, RLE, placement, story, variable, event, predicate, action, sequence, rule, feature, execution, diagnostic, and recovery boundary in plain language. Nominal ScenarioId, ContentId, MessageId, LogicalPath, and tag types prevent cross-domain identity mixing; closed-model probes construct and exhaustively match every enum family; the negative fixture proves ContentId cannot satisfy ScenarioId. The canonical minimal map and model boundary reject target-specific imports and raw/untyped seams. The exact toolchain now contains 39 commands with this proof separately visible before the unchanged 12-request Caxecraft domain lane. Verified by test:caxecraft-scenario-model, 20 shard tests, 108 governance tests, typed-boundary coverage over 421 owned Haxe files, formatter, all-sources, git diff checks, and the fast Eval/split/package/unity O2 Caxecraft differential.

title: Make exhaustive local tests resource-adaptive and resumable
description: The package-layout publication exposed two avoidable feedback-loop costs. A staged cross-cutting change reran the exact 38-command matrix after equivalent focused/full evidence had already passed, then a two-worker run on a host with load 30-55 timed out one warm Caxecraft Haxe request after the other three shards passed. Local scheduling currently defaults to two workers regardless of host capacity, while a clean four-worker baseline is materially faster. Improve throughput without dropping or weakening any semantic, native, sanitizer, determinism, snapshot, policy, or security evidence.
design: Separate user build latency from suite multiplication. Select a documented resource-aware default: use up to four isolated shard workers on a sufficiently idle multicore host, reduce concurrency under measured contention, and retain an explicit --jobs or HXC_TEST_JOBS override. Add fail-closed content-addressed evidence for the local hook: key each successful shard by the staged Git tree plus complete command/shard definition, reviewed toolchain locks and identities, relevant environment, and runner/hook versions. A retry may reuse only matching successful shards and must rerun every failed, missing, malformed, expired, or mismatched record. Store local evidence outside tracked artifacts and never let it replace clean hosted CI. Preserve deterministic ordered reporting and add phase/request timing for Caxecraft cold, warm, backend, and native work.
acceptance_criteria: An idle machine with at least eight logical CPUs selects and documents a higher default concurrency, while a deliberately loaded test selects a conservative worker count; explicit 1-4 overrides remain validated. Unit tests cover portable load-probe fallback, malformed overrides, worker bounds, and deterministic scheduling. A failed four-shard run that has three valid success records reruns only the failed shard when the staged tree, locks, tool identities, commands, and relevant environment are byte-identical. Any source/index/lock/runner/tool/environment drift invalidates the appropriate evidence and fails closed to execution. Security, whitespace, formatting, Beads export, and cheap governance checks always run and are never cached. Hosted CI still performs clean isolated shards. Comparable timing reports show before/after cold, warm, and contended results; docs explain normal one-build latency separately from exhaustive QA multiplication. npm run test:governance, toolchain-shard tests, focused hook tests, a clean four-worker matrix, a contended fallback probe, and remote Governance pass.
notes:
Implemented resource-adaptive 1/2/4-worker local shard selection with strict 1-4 CLI/environment overrides; exact 24-hour local resume receipts keyed by staged tree, worktree/untracked inputs, complete registry, locks, npm-selected tool identities (including cc), full inherited environment, host, runner, and hook; owned atomic evidence storage; deterministic executed/reused reports; and embedded Caxecraft phase timing with failure-prefix preservation. Hosted CI remains cold and uncached; pre-commit keeps Beads export, formatting, path/whitespace, secret, governance, and native checks outside reuse. Focused evidence: 20 toolchain-runner tests, 3 Cax timing/schema tests, 105-test governance suite, CI/security/governance policy checks, and a forced four-worker 38-command functional matrix passed. The contended matrix completed in 546,791ms after a load 11.16/12 probe (automatic selection correctly chose one); Cax phases were backend 150,698ms, cold 206,013ms, warm 173,932ms, snapshots 7,817ms, native 6,178ms. This is documented as contention evidence, not a speed claim. Clean hosted after timing and remote Governance remain required before closure.
Clean hosted evidence is complete: Governance run 29757235207 passed every required job and the aggregate gate in about 13m26s. Uploaded exact shard reports recorded contracts 425,071ms, lowering-objects 355,718ms, lowering-semantics 242,829ms, and Caxecraft 782,781ms. Caxecraft's 12-request phase report recorded backend-render 202,155ms, cold-determinism 269,783ms, warm-server 266,312ms, snapshot-validation 11,808ms, and native-build-run 30,652ms. The documentation states plainly that this slower sample is instrumentation/baseline evidence, not a speed claim. Final local governance passed 108 tests, exact 38-command partition checks, security/governance policies, and diff checks.
close_reason: Completed resource-adaptive 1/2/4-worker local scheduling, strict 1-4 overrides, exact 24-hour fail-closed resume receipts, deterministic executed/reused reporting, and complete Caxecraft phase/request timing. A forced four-worker local matrix passed all 38 commands; a contended automatic probe chose one worker; immediate exact retry reused only four valid shard receipts while uncached policy/native gates reran. Local governance passed 108 tests. Clean hosted Governance run 29757235207 passed every job and aggregate in about 13m26s, with uploaded exact shard and 12-request Caxecraft phase reports. Hosted CI remains cold and cannot consume local receipts.

title: Reduce span-lowering cold compiler process amplification
description: The exact toolchain timing report identifies test:span-lowering as the local critical-path command: 285042ms in the clean four-worker sample, or 64% of the lowering-semantics shard. Static inspection shows the runner forces HAXE_NO_SERVER=1 and launches 87 independent Haxe processes before its native matrix. Preserve the full typed, negative, determinism, profile/build, bounds-abort, GCC/Clang, optimization, sanitizer, and no-hxrt evidence while reducing duplicated compiler startup and setup work.
design: Instrument internal phases first. Batch compatible negative/profile fixtures or produce a validated multi-case report when one compiler invocation can retain exact source-anchored diagnostics. Reuse generated projects only when all semantic inputs and expected configuration coordinates are proven identical. Keep a separate warm compiler-server lifecycle/reentrancy lane; do not replace cold evidence entirely. Parallelize only cases with isolated output roots and bounded jobs. Compare cold before/after samples on the same runner class.
acceptance_criteria: A machine-readable phase/invocation report accounts for the current work; all existing span-lowering semantic, negative, determinism, profile/build, bounds-abort, native optimization/sanitizer, and zero-hxrt assertions remain executable; forced-cold Haxe process count and comparable wall time are materially reduced; focused span, toolchain-shard, snapshot-owner, governance, and hosted CI gates pass; documentation records the new topology and any retained cold/warm lanes.
notes:
Implemented the guarded 54-request topology: three cold six-coordinate typed-report matrices (normal/repeat/reversed input), 36 exact negative diagnostics, two configuration diagnostics, seven production/repeat builds, and six bounds-matrix builds. Eight requests remain cold, 46 use one isolated serial Haxe server, and total compiler loads fall from 87 to 9. A typed native selector keeps all four local/parameter upper/negative bounds paths independently executable per profile/build; each marked path aborts under strict C and links without hxrt. The path-free schema-1 timing report records phases and invocations. Local full samples were 145336ms before bounds batching and 91226ms after; the canonical lowering-semantics shard then passed in 251888ms with span at 89064ms. Focused span, format, snapshot catalog, independent cold span snapshot, exact shard, governance, and full native smoke pass. Pinned-Haxe cached TypedExpr source-position drift remains explicit follow-up haxe_c-xge.27; exact HxcIR report renders stay cold instead of normalizing it away. Hosted CI is pending the implementation push.
Hosted evidence is green for implementation commit 75ea5aa. Governance run 29705038807 passed every job; its lowering-semantics shard passed in 4m56s, including the 54-request span suite and retained native checks. Cold snapshot audit run 29705038799 independently re-rendered every registered snapshot and passed in 7m19s. Run URLs: https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29705038807 and https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29705038799.
close_reason: Reduced compiler loads from 87 cold processes to 9 total loads while preserving the complete semantic, diagnostic, determinism, profile, bounds-abort, native, sanitizer, and no-hxrt evidence. Focused, exhaustive local, Governance, and independent cold-snapshot gates all pass. Warm-cache source-anchor drift is retained as explicit follow-up haxe_c-xge.27 rather than hidden by this optimization.

title: De-duplicate pinned-toolchain snapshot regeneration without weakening coverage
description: The serial test:toolchain command runs every owning compiler suite and then snapshots:check regenerates the same 30 suites again. Governance run 29696206056 spent the final minutes repeating registered snapshot renders after every preceding suite, and the aggregate exceeded 30 minutes. Reduce CI feedback time while preserving central ownership, complete-suite registration, exact byte comparison, and every focused semantic/native gate.
design: Measure per-suite wall time and classify what the final central snapshot pass uniquely proves. Introduce the smallest fail-closed separation between ownership/registration validation and artifact regeneration, or reuse cryptographically identified outputs only when provenance makes that equivalent. Do not simply delete snapshots:check, hide drift, weaken deterministic regeneration, or rely on cache state.
acceptance_criteria: A reproducible before/after timing report identifies the duplicated work; the pinned-toolchain job avoids redundant compiler renders while still failing on an unregistered suite, stale snapshot, missing owner, and changed generated bytes; focused runners and the central snapshot command retain their standalone contracts; cold CI completes with meaningful headroom; governance and all platform jobs pass.
notes:
Phase 1 is shipped in commit a5d2fe3: fast feedback is a repository directive; GitHub partitions the canonical exhaustive sequence into five isolated exact-coverage shards behind a fail-closed aggregate; the serial local reference remains intact. Governance run 29698408759 passed all 17 jobs in 8m07s. Shards: lowering-objects 4m48s, Caxecraft 6m14s, contracts 6m35s, snapshots 7m17s, lowering-semantics 7m59s; aggregate 3s. This is one cold hosted sample, not a p95. Remaining scope is real duplicate snapshot-compute removal, per-suite timing evidence, and safe local concurrency classification; parallel execution alone is not de-duplication.
Implementation candidate now replaces the integrated full snapshot replay with a 0.55s fail-closed catalog/owner check, retains a weekly/change-triggered/manual cold audit, emits path-free per-command timing JSON, and admits four isolated local shards with a conservative two-worker default. A clean four-worker run passed all 38 commands in 444794ms; critical path was lowering-semantics, where span-lowering took 285042ms. The runner forces 87 cold Haxe starts; discovered follow-up haxe_c-xge.26 owns consolidation without coverage loss.
Hosted acceptance: Governance 29702208826 passed all 16 jobs in 8m25s. Uploaded shard timings: contracts 392452ms (project-emitter 152450ms), lowering-objects 371042ms (constructor 64397ms), lowering-semantics 462433ms (span-lowering 289947ms), Caxecraft 400790ms (full suite 400176ms), and snapshot catalog 614ms. Independent Cold snapshot audit 29702208854 re-rendered all 30 suites successfully in 7m14s. The required path therefore removed the old 7m17 snapshot shard while retaining cold authority. haxe_c-xge.26 owns the next measured bottleneck.
close_reason: Focused suites retain exact snapshot comparison, the integrated lane now proves one-owner catalog coverage in under a second, the independent cold audit retains full regeneration authority, bounded local concurrency and deterministic timings are executable, and both hosted workflows passed.

title: Add package-coalesced generated-C layout
description: Complete the remaining package-level output strategy required by HXC-COMP-011. The current compiler intentionally implements source-shaped per-module split output and unity output only; package coalescing must reuse the same validated HxcIR, representation, naming, runtime, declaration, and ownership plans rather than creating a parallel lowering path.
design: Add a third closed project-layout variant only after module and unity behavior is stable. Coalesce modules by normalized Haxe package, preserve hard complete-type ordering versus forward-declarable declaration edges, keep deterministic portable paths and exact manifests, and retain output-ownership stale cleanup when switching among all modes.
acceptance_criteria: A multi-package fixture and Caxecraft emit deterministic package-grouped headers/sources; all standalone headers are strict-C11 valid; mixed hard/soft type cycles remain legal; GCC/Clang O0/O2 and Eval traces match module split and unity; cold/reverse/locale/warm output is byte-stable; hxc.manifest.json and stale ownership enumerate exactly the selected package artifacts; docs and requirement mappings identify package mode as implemented only after these gates pass.
notes: Implemented the third HXC-COMP-011 package layout as deterministic file assignment over the existing HxcIR, declaration, symbol, runtime, and ownership plans. Package headers/sources preserve hard complete-type ordering, soft forward edges, exact manifests, standalone strict C11 use, stale cleanup, source-package artifact ownership, and source-module function ownership. Split and unity remain unchanged; unity still emits one src/program.c. Review findings were resolved by scoping package-path collisions to package mode and by exercising real Caxecraft package output across cold, reversed-order, alternate-locale, and warm-server builds. Focused recursive safety-unit coverage now proves split and package non-return partitions. Evidence: npm run test:project-layout -- --toolchain auto; npm run test:function-lowering; npm run test:caxecraft-domain:full; npm run test:governance; npm run snapshots:catalog; git diff --check. The full Caxecraft lane passed Eval parity, 32 seeds, exact snapshots, strict O0/O2 and ASan+UBSan for split/package/unity, zero hxrt/allocation symbols, and maintainability parity.
close_reason: Completed HXC-COMP-011 package-coalesced output on commit dcbb2e30227a201ae9ed851a76582f288e5a5fa0. Package, split, and unity layouts share the validated HxcIR, naming, declaration, runtime, and ownership plans; unity still emits one src/program.c. Exact manifests, stale cleanup, standalone strict-C11 headers, hard/soft dependency cycles, non-returning partitions, Caxecraft cold/reverse/locale/warm determinism, Eval parity, O0/O2, and ASan+UBSan all passed. Remote Governance 29729525446 and Cold snapshot audit 29729525464 passed.

title: Add Ivvy as Haxirio companion
description:
Add Ivvy, an original voxel Siamese-cat companion inspired by the family cat reference supplied privately by the user. Ivvy follows Haxirio through Adventure, remains near the player without obstructing building or combat, recovers deterministically when navigation fails, and helps through readable bounded abilities: point toward nearby secrets and the Tideweave trail, warn about enemies, retrieve small eligible drops, and provide a modest purr-heal at checkpoints. Underwater, Ivvy uses a visible bubble collar and follows only routes that are safe for the companion.

Ivvy is a helper and friend, never an enemy target, disposable resource, or fail condition. Damage cannot kill Ivvy; dangerous states cause retreat, cover, or recovery. A stay/follow interaction lets the player control positioning. The design should feel warm and recognizable to a child without copying the private photo into the repository or making identity claims beyond the generated stylized asset.

The companion is authored through the same CaxeMap entity placement and CaxeFlow state/action surfaces used by other content. Simulation remains renderer-independent fixed-step Haxe; raylib owns presentation only. English and es-MX names, prompts, dialogue, accessibility descriptions, and tutorial text are required.
design: Use a closed typed companion state machine such as Follow, Stay, CatchUp, PointSecret, Warn, Retrieve, Purr, Swim, Cover, and Sleep, with explicit bounded transitions and no general pathfinding engine. Prefer line-of-sight, local steering, last-safe-position recovery, and a deterministic catch-up warp hidden behind a short visual effect when distance or stuck ticks exceed declared limits. Abilities use cooldowns and typed targets; they reveal hints rather than solving puzzles automatically. Ivvy state, stay position, relationship progress, and enabled abilities serialize by stable language-neutral IDs. The user photo is a private visual reference only; retain no copy or biometric metadata. Record the derived atlas prompt, generation artifact ID, processing, dimensions, and hash.
acceptance_criteria:
- Ivvy can be placed through CaxeMap, appears beside Haxirio in Adventure, and has explicit Follow and Stay interaction with clear HUD/audio-free feedback.
- Fixed-step traces cover every companion state, follow distance bands, obstacle steering, stuck recovery, checkpoint respawn, save/load, player death/retry, both castle routes, and no-soft-lock behavior.
- Ivvy is never targetable by hostile damage, never dies, never consumes quest-critical items, never blocks a voxel placement or narrow route, and safely retreats or recovers from hazards.
- Secret pointing, enemy warning, eligible-drop retrieval, and checkpoint purr-heal are bounded by typed ranges, cooldowns, priorities, and deterministic tie-breaking; abilities hint and assist without completing the suit code, bridge puzzle, boss, or objectives for the player.
- The Tideweave sequence includes a visible bubble-collar state and safe underwater follow/recovery behavior; entering water without an admitted safe route never strands Ivvy or the player.
- English and es-MX catalogs cover the name, interaction prompts, tutorial, journal/help text, accessibility description, and any Ivvy dialogue or status; gameplay and save identity do not depend on localized strings.
- Eval and generated C traces match. Headless real-raylib e2e, semantic UI snapshots, pinned software-render goldens, sanitizers, allocation/resource budgets, and generated-C inspection pass before showcase closure.
- The checked-in Ivvy atlas is original, records prompt/generation/processing/hash provenance, and does not include or redistribute the private reference photo.
notes: The initial 4x4 Ivvy atlas is checked in and byte-pinned as original stylized art. Its generation record names the private family-cat photo only as a non-retained reference and stores no path or metadata. No companion behavior is implemented yet; the issue remains the owner for safe fixed-step mechanics, localization, CaxeMap/CaxeFlow integration, and e2e.

title: Ship Caxecraft in English and Spanish
description:
Make English and Spanish first-class supported Caxecraft languages across the menu, Creative mode, Adventure story/dialogue/objectives/journal, editor, CaxeFlow visual rule cards, tutorials, settings, errors, and accessibility text.
